**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.18330v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.18330v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.18330v1)

**Categories**: cs.AI, cs.HC, cs.LG, cs.MA

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Persistent Large Language Model (LLM) agents expose a critical governance gap in memory management. Standard Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) frameworks treat memory as passive storage, lacking mechanisms to resolve contradictions, enforce privacy, or prevent outdated information ("zombie memories") from contaminating the context window.   We introduce MemArchitect, a governance layer that decouples memory lifecycle management from model weights. MemArchitect enforces explicit, rule-based policies, including memory decay, conflict resolution, and privacy controls.   We demonstrate that governed memory consistently outperforms unmanaged memory in agentic settings, highlighting the necessity of structured memory governance for reliable and safe autonomous systems.

持久性大语言模型 (LLM) 代理暴露了内存管理中的一个关键治理差距。标准检索增强生成（RAG）框架将内存视为被动存储，缺乏解决矛盾、加强隐私或防止过时信息（“僵尸记忆”）污染上下文窗口的机制。   我们引入了 MemArchitect，一个将内存生命周期管理与模型权重分离的治理层。 MemArchitect 强制执行明确的、基于规则的策略，包括内存衰减、冲突解决和隐私控制。   我们证明，在代理环境中，受治理的内存始终优于非托管内存，这凸显了结构化内存治理对于可靠和安全的自治系统的必要性。

</details>

---

## 32. EDM-ARS: A Domain-Specific Multi-Agent System for Automated Educational Data Mining Research / EDM-ARS：用于自动化教育数据挖掘研究的特定领域多代理系统

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.18273v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.18273v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.18273v1)

**Categories**: cs.AI

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

In this technical report, we present the Educational Data Mining Automated Research System (EDM-ARS), a domain-specific multi-agent pipeline that automates end-to-end educational data mining (EDM) research. We conceptualize EDM-ARS as a general framework for domain-aware automated research pipelines, where educational expertise is embedded into each stage of the research lifecycle. As a first instantiation of this framework, we focus on predictive modeling tasks. Within this scope, EDM-ARS orchestrates five specialized LLM-powered agents (ProblemFormulator, DataEngineer, Analyst, Critic, and Writer) through a state-machine coordinator that supports revision loops, checkpoint-based recovery, and sandboxed code execution. Given a research prompt and a dataset, EDM-ARS produces a complete LaTeX manuscript with real Semantic Scholar citations, validated machine learning analyses, and automated methodological peer review. We also provide a detailed description of the system architecture, the three-tier data registry design that encodes educational domain expertise, the specification of each agent, the inter-agent communication protocol, and mechanisms for error-handling and self-correction. Finally, we discuss current limitations, including single-dataset scope and formulaic paper output, and outline a phased roadmap toward causal inference, transfer learning, psychometric, and multi-dataset generalization. EDM-ARS is released as an open-source project to support the educational research community.

在这份技术报告中，我们介绍了教育数据挖掘自动化研究系统（EDM-ARS），这是一个特定领域的多代理管道，可自动执行端到端教育数据挖掘（EDM）研究。我们将 EDM-ARS 概念化为领域感知自动化研究管道的通用框架，其中教育专业知识嵌入到研究生命周期的每个阶段。作为该框架的第一个实例，我们专注于预测建模任务。在此范围内，EDM-ARS 通过支持修订循环、基于检查点的恢复和沙盒代码执行的状态机协调器协调五个由 LLM 驱动的专门代理（ProblemFormulator、DataEngineer、Analyst、Critic 和 Writer）。给定研究提示和数据集，EDM-ARS 会生成完整的 LaTeX 手稿，其中包含真实的语义学者引用、经过验证的机器学习分析和自动方法同行评审。我们还提供了系统架构、编码教育领域专业知识的三层数据注册表设计、每个代理的规范、代理间通信协议以及错误处理和自我纠正机制的详细描述。最后，我们讨论了当前的局限性，包括单数据集范围和公式化的论文输出，并概述了因果推理、迁移学习、心理测量和多数据集泛化的分阶段路线图。 EDM-ARS 作为开源项目发布，以支持教育研究社区。

</details>

---

## 33. SG-CoT: An Ambiguity-Aware Robotic Planning Framework using Scene Graph Representations / SG-CoT：使用场景图表示的模糊感知机器人规划框架

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.18271v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.18271v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.18271v1)

**Categories**: cs.RO

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Ambiguity poses a major challenge to large language models (LLMs) used as robotic planners. In this letter, we present Scene Graph-Chain-of-Thought (SG-CoT), a two-stage framework where LLMs iteratively query a scene graph representation of the environment to detect and clarify ambiguities. First, a structured scene graph representation of the environment is constructed from input observations, capturing objects, their attributes, and relationships with other objects. Second, the LLM is equipped with retrieval functions to query portions of the scene graph that are relevant to the provided instruction. This grounds the reasoning process of the LLM in the observation, increasing the reliability of robotic planners under ambiguous situations. SG-CoT also allows the LLM to identify the source of ambiguity and pose a relevant disambiguation question to the user or another robot. Extensive experimentation demonstrates that SG-CoT consistently outperforms prior methods, with a minimum of 10% improvement in question accuracy and a minimum success rate increase of 4% in single-agent and 15% in multi-agent environments, validating its effectiveness for more generalizable robot planning.

歧义性给用作机器人规划器的大型语言模型（LLM）带来了重大挑战。在这封信中，我们提出了场景图思想链（SG-CoT），这是一个两阶段框架，其中法学硕士迭代查询环境的场景图表示以检测和澄清歧义。首先，根据输入观察、捕获对象、它们的属性以及与其他对象的关系构建环境的结构化场景图表示。其次，法学硕士配备了检索功能来查询与所提供的指令相关的场景图部分。这将法学硕士的推理过程建立在观察中，提高了机器人规划者在模糊情况下的可靠性。 SG-CoT 还允许法学硕士识别歧义的来源并向用户或其他机器人提出相关的消歧问题。大量实验表明，SG-CoT 始终优于先前的方法，问题准确率至少提高了 10%，单智能体环境中的成功率至少提高了 4%，多智能体环境中的成功率提高了 15%，验证了其对于更通用的机器人规划的有效性。

</details>

---

## 34. GoalVLM: VLM-driven Object Goal Navigation for Multi-Agent System / GoalVLM：VLM 驱动的多代理系统对象目标导航

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.18210v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.18210v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.18210v1)

**Categories**: cs.RO

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Object-goal navigation has traditionally been limited to ground robots with closed-set object vocabularies. Existing multi-agent approaches depend on precomputed probabilistic graphs tied to fixed category sets, precluding generalization to novel goals at test time.   We present GoalVLM, a cooperative multi-agent framework for zero-shot, open-vocabulary object navigation. GoalVLM integrates a Vision-Language Model (VLM) directly into the decision loop, SAM3 for text-prompted detection and segmentation, and SpaceOM for spatial reasoning, enabling agents to interpret free-form language goals and score frontiers via zero-shot semantic priors without retraining. Each agent builds a BEV semantic map from depth-projected voxel splatting, while a Goal Projector back-projects detections through calibrated depth into the map for reliable goal localization. A constraint-guided reasoning layer evaluates frontiers through a structured prompt chain (scene captioning, room-type classification, perception gating, multi-frontier ranking), injecting commonsense priors into exploration.   We evaluate GoalVLM on GOAT-Bench val_unseen (360 multi-subtask episodes, 1032 sequential object-goal subtasks, HM3D scenes), where each episode requires navigating to a chain of 5-7 open-vocabulary targets. GoalVLM with N=2 agents achieves 55.8% subtask SR and 18.3% SPL, competitive with state-of-the-art methods while requiring no task-specific training. Ablation studies confirm the contributions of VLM-guided frontier reasoning and depth-projected goal localization.

目标目标导航传统上仅限于具有封闭目标词汇表的地面机器人。现有的多智能体方法依赖于与固定类别集相关的预先计算的概率图，从而无法在测试时泛化到新目标。   我们提出了 GoalVLM，一个用于零样本、开放词汇对象导航的协作多智能体框架。 GoalVLM 将视觉语言模型 (VLM) 直接集成到决策循环中，将 SAM3 用于文本提示检测和分割，将 SpaceOM 用于空间推理，使代理能够解释自由形式的语言目标，并通过零样本语义先验对边界进行评分，而无需重新训练。每个智能体都根据深度投影体素泼溅构建 BEV 语义图，而目标投影仪则通过校准深度将检测结果反向投影到地图中，以实现可靠的目标定位。约束引导推理层通过结构化提示链（场景字幕、房间类型分类、感知门控、多前沿排名）评估前沿，将常识先验注入探索中。   我们在 GOAT-Bench val_unseen（360 个多子任务情节、1032 个连续对象目标子任务、HM3D 场景）上评估 GoalVLM，其中每个情节需要导航到 5-7 个开放词汇目标的链。具有 N=2 个智能体的 GoalVLM 实现了 55.8% 的子任务 SR 和 18.3% SPL，与最先进的方法相媲美，同时不需要特定任务的培训。消融研究证实了 VLM 引导的前沿推理和深度投影目标定位的贡献。

</details>

---

## 35. Access Controlled Website Interaction for Agentic AI with Delegated Critical Tasks / 代理 AI 与委派关键任务的访问受控网站交互

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.18197v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.18197v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.18197v1)

**Categories**: cs.AI, cs.CR, cs.NI

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Recent studies reveal gaps in delegating critical tasks to agentic AI that accesses websites on the user's behalf, primarily due to limited access control mechanisms on websites designed for agentic AI. In response, we propose a design of website-based interaction for AI agents with fine-grained access control for delegated critical tasks. Our approach encompasses a website design and implementation, as well as modifications to the access grant protocols in an open-source authorization service to tailor it to agentic AI, with delegated critical tasks on the website. The evaluation of our approach demonstrates the capabilities of our access-controlled website used by AI agents.

最近的研究揭示了在将关键任务委托给代表用户访问网站的代理人工智能方面存在差距，这主要是由于为代理人工智能设计的网站上的访问控制机制有限。作为回应，我们提出了一种基于网站的人工智能代理交互设计，为委派的关键任务提供细粒度的访问控制。我们的方法包括网站设计和实施，以及对开源授权服务中的访问授权协议的修改，以适应代理人工智能，并在网站上委派关键任务。对我们方法的评估展示了人工智能代理使用的访问控制网站的功能。

</details>

---

## 36. Don't Vibe Code, Do Skele-Code: Interactive No-Code Notebooks for Subject Matter Experts to Build Lower-Cost Agentic Workflows / 不要Vibe Code，Do Skele-Code：交互式无代码笔记本，供主题专家构建低成本代理工作流程

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.18122v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.18122v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.18122v1)

**Categories**: cs.AI, cs.HC, cs.PL, eess.SY

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Skele-Code is a natural-language and graph-based interface for building workflows with AI agents, designed especially for less or non-technical users. It supports incremental, interactive notebook-style development, and each step is converted to code with a required set of functions and behavior to enable incremental building of workflows. Agents are invoked only for code generation and error recovery, not orchestration or task execution. This agent-supported, but code-first approach to workflows, along with the context-engineering used in Skele-Code, can help reduce token costs compared to the multi-agent system approach to executing workflows. Skele-Code produces modular, easily extensible, and shareable workflows. The generated workflows can also be used as skills by agents, or as steps in other workflows.

Skele-Code 是一种自然语言和基于图形的界面，用于使用 AI 代理构建工作流程，专为技术含量较低或非技术用户而设计。它支持增量式、交互式笔记本式开发，并且每个步骤都转换为具有所需功能和行为集的代码，以实现工作流的增量构建。调用代理仅用于代码生成和错误恢复，而不是编排或任务执行。与执行工作流的多代理系统方法相比，这种由代理支持但代码优先的工作流方法以及 Skele-Code 中使用的上下文工程可以帮助降低令牌成本。 Skele-Code 产生模块化、易于扩展和可共享的工作流程。生成的工作流还可以被代理用作技能，或用作其他工作流中的步骤。

</details>

---

## 37. Insight-V++: Towards Advanced Long-Chain Visual Reasoning with Multimodal Large Language Models / Insight-V++：利用多模态大语言模型进行高级长链视觉推理

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.18118v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.18118v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.18118v1)

**Categories**: cs.CV, cs.AI, cs.LG

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved remarkable reliability and advanced capabilities through extended test-time reasoning. However, extending these capabilities to Multi-modal Large Language Models (MLLMs) remains a significant challenge due to a critical scarcity of high-quality, long-chain reasoning data and optimized training pipelines. To bridge this gap, we present a unified multi-agent visual reasoning framework that systematically evolves from our foundational image-centric model, Insight-V, into a generalized spatial-temporal architecture, Insight-V++. We first propose a scalable data generation pipeline equipped with multi-granularity assessment that autonomously synthesizes structured, complex reasoning trajectories across image and video domains without human intervention. Recognizing that directly supervising MLLMs with such intricate data yields sub-optimal results, we design a dual-agent architecture comprising a reasoning agent to execute extensive analytical chains, and a summary agent to critically evaluate and distill final outcomes. While our initial framework utilized Direct Preference Optimization (DPO), its off-policy nature fundamentally constrained reinforcement learning potential. To overcome these limitations, particularly for long-horizon video understanding, Insight-V++ introduces two novel algorithms, ST-GRPO and J-GRPO, which enhance spatial-temporal reasoning and improve evaluative robustness. Crucially, by leveraging reliable feedback from the summary agent, we guide an iterative reasoning path generation process, retraining the entire multi-agent system in a continuous, self-improving loop. Extensive experiments on base models like LLaVA-NeXT and Qwen2.5-VL demonstrate significant performance gains across challenging image and video reasoning benchmarks while preserving strong capabilities on traditional perception-focused tasks.

大型语言模型 (LLM) 通过延长测试时间推理实现了卓越的可靠性和先进的功能。然而，由于高质量、长链推理数据和优化的训练管道的严重缺乏，将这些功能扩展到多模态大型语言模型（MLLM）仍然是一个重大挑战。为了弥补这一差距，我们提出了一个统一的多智能体视觉推理框架，该框架系统地从我们以图像为中心的基础模型 Insight-V 演变为广义的时空架构 Insight-V++。我们首先提出了一种配备多粒度评估的可扩展数据生成管道，可以在无需人工干预的情况下自动合成跨图像和视频领域的结构化、复杂的推理轨迹。认识到用如此复杂的数据直接监督 MLLM 会产生次优结果，我们设计了一种双代理架构，其中包括一个用于执行广泛分析链的推理代理和一个用于严格评估和提取最终结果的摘要代理。虽然我们最初的框架利用了直接偏好优化（DPO），但其离策略性质从根本上限制了强化学习的潜力。为了克服这些限制，特别是对于长视距视频理解，Insight-V++ 引入了两种新颖的算法：ST-GRPO 和 J-GRPO，它们增强了时空推理并提高了评估的鲁棒性。至关重要的是，通过利用摘要代理的可靠反馈，我们指导迭代推理路径生成过程，在连续的自我改进循环中重新训练整个多代理系统。在 LLaVA-NeXT 和 Qwen2.5-VL 等基础模型上进行的大量实验表明，在具有挑战性的图像和视频推理基准测试中，性能显着提升，同时保留了传统的以感知为中心的任务的强大功能。

</details>

---

## 38. TDAD: Test-Driven Agentic Development - Reducing Code Regressions in AI Coding Agents via Graph-Based Impact Analysis / TDAD：测试驱动的代理开发 - 通过基于图的影响分析减少人工智能编码代理中的代码回归

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.17973v2](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17973v2) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17973v2)

**Categories**: cs.SE, cs.AI

**Code**: https://github.com/pepealonso95/TDAD.

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

AI coding agents can resolve real-world software issues, yet they frequently introduce regressions -- breaking tests that previously passed. Current benchmarks focus almost exclusively on resolution rate, leaving regression behavior under-studied. This paper presents TDAD (Test-Driven Agentic Development), an open-source tool that performs pre-change impact analysis for AI coding agents. TDAD builds a dependency map between source code and tests so that before committing a patch, the agent knows which tests to verify and can self-correct. The map is delivered as a lightweight agent skill -- a static text file the agent queries at runtime. Evaluated on SWE-bench Verified with two open-weight models running on consumer hardware (Qwen3-Coder 30B, 100 instances; Qwen3.5-35B-A3B, 25 instances), TDAD reduced regressions by 70% (6.08% to 1.82%) compared to a vanilla baseline. In contrast, adding TDD procedural instructions without targeted test context increased regressions to 9.94% -- worse than no intervention at all. When deployed as an agent skill with a different model and framework, TDAD improved issue-resolution rate from 24% to 32%, confirming that surfacing contextual information outperforms prescribing procedural workflows. All code, data, and logs are publicly available at https://github.com/pepealonso95/TDAD.

人工智能编码代理可以解决现实世界的软件问题，但它们经常引入回归——破坏之前通过的测试。当前的基准几乎只关注解决率，而对回归行为的研究不足。本文介绍了 TDAD（测试驱动代理开发），这是一种开源工具，可为 AI 编码代理执行变更前影响分析。 TDAD 在源代码和测试之间构建依赖关系图，以便在提交补丁之前，代理知道要验证哪些测试并可以自我纠正。该地图作为轻量级代理技能提供——代理在运行时查询的静态文本文件。在 SWE-bench 上进行评估，使用在消费类硬件上运行的两个开放权重模型（Qwen3-Coder 30B，100 个实例；Qwen3.5-35B-A3B，25 个实例）进行验证，与普通基线相比，TDAD 将回归减少了 70%（6.08% 至 1.82%）。相比之下，在没有目标测试上下文的情况下添加 TDD 程序指令会将回归率提高到 9.94%——比根本不干预更糟糕。当使用不同的模型和框架部署为代理技能时，TDAD 将问题解决率从 24% 提高到 32%，证实显示上下文信息优于规定的程序工作流程。所有代码、数据和日志均可在 https://github.com/pepealonso95/TDAD 上公开获取。

</details>

---

## 39. When Only the Final Text Survives: Implicit Execution Tracing for Multi-Agent Attribution / 当只有最终文本幸存时：多代理归因的隐式执行跟踪

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.17445v2](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17445v2) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17445v2)

**Categories**: cs.AI, cs.CL

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

When a multi-agent system produces an incorrect or harmful answer, who is accountable if execution logs and agent identifiers are unavailable? Multi-agent language systems increasingly rely on structured interactions such as delegation and iterative refinement, yet the final output often obscures the underlying interaction topology and agent contributions. We introduce IET (Implicit Execution Tracing), a metadata-independent framework that enables token-level attribution directly from generated text and a simple mechanism for interaction topology reconstruction. During generation, agent-specific keyed signals are embedded into the token distribution, transforming the text into a self-describing execution trace detectable only with a secret key. At detection time, a transition-aware scoring method identifies agent handover points and reconstructs the interaction graph. Experiments show that IET recovers agent segments and coordination structure with high accuracy while preserving generation quality, enabling privacy-preserving auditing for multi-agent language systems.

当多代理系统产生不正确或有害的答案时，如果执行日志和代理标识符不可用，谁负责？多智能体语言系统越来越依赖于结构化交互，例如委托和迭代细化，但最终的输出往往掩盖了底层交互拓扑和智能体贡献。我们引入了 IET（隐式执行跟踪），这是一个独立于元数据的框架，可以直接从生成的文本中进行令牌级归因，以及用于交互拓扑重建的简单机制。在生成过程中，特定于代理的键控信号被嵌入到令牌分布中，将文本转换为只能使用密钥检测到的自描述执行跟踪。在检测时，转换感知评分方法识别代理切换点并重建交互图。实验表明，IET 可以高精度地恢复智能体片段和协调结构，同时保持生成质量，从而实现多智能体语言系统的隐私保护审计。

</details>

---



</details>

<details><summary><b>2026-03-19 (26 papers)</b></summary>

# arXiv Agent Papers - 2026-03-19

**Paper Count**: 26

## 1. TDAD: Test-Driven Agentic Development - Reducing Code Regressions in AI Coding Agents via Graph-Based Impact Analysis / TDAD：测试驱动的代理开发 - 通过基于图的影响分析减少人工智能编码代理中的代码回归

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.17973v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17973v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17973v1)

**Categories**: cs.SE, cs.AI

**Code**: https://github.com/pepealonso95/TDAD.

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

AI coding agents can resolve real-world software issues, yet they frequently introduce regressions, breaking tests that previously passed. Current benchmarks focus almost exclusively on resolution rate, leaving regression behavior under-studied. This paper presents TDAD (Test-Driven Agentic Development), an open-source tool and benchmark methodology that combines abstract-syntax-tree (AST) based code-test graph construction with weighted impact analysis to surface the tests most likely affected by a proposed change. Evaluated on SWE-bench Verified with two local models (Qwen3-Coder 30B on 100 instances and Qwen3.5-35B-A3B on 25 instances), TDAD's GraphRAG workflow reduced test-level regressions by 70% (6.08% to 1.82%) and improved resolution from 24% to 32% when deployed as an agent skill. A surprising finding is that TDD prompting alone increased regressions (9.94%), revealing that smaller models benefit more from contextual information (which tests to verify) than from procedural instructions (how to do TDD). An autonomous auto-improvement loop raised resolution from 12% to 60% on a 10-instance subset with 0% regression. These findings suggest that for AI agent tool design, surfacing contextual information outperforms prescribing procedural workflows. All code, data, and logs are publicly available at https://github.com/pepealonso95/TDAD.

人工智能编码代理可以解决现实世界的软件问题，但它们经常引入回归，破坏之前通过的测试。当前的基准几乎只关注解决率，而对回归行为的研究不足。本文介绍了 TDAD（测试驱动代理开发），这是一种开源工具和基准测试方法，它将基于抽象语法树 (AST) 的代码测试图构建与加权影响分析相结合，以显示最有可能受提议变更影响的测试。在 SWE-bench 上使用两个本地模型（100 个实例上的 Qwen3-Coder 30B 和 25 个实例上的 Qwen3.5-35B-A3B）进行评估，TDAD 的 GraphRAG 工作流程将测试级回归减少了 70%（6.08% 到 1.82%），并且当部署为代理技能时，分辨率从 24% 提高到 32%。一个令人惊讶的发现是，单独 TDD 提示会增加回归（9.94％），这表明较小的模型从上下文信息（测试以验证）中受益更多，而不是从程序指令（如何进行 TDD）中受益。自主自动改进循环将 10 个实例子集的分辨率从 12% 提高到 60%，回归率为 0%。这些发现表明，对于人工智能代理工具设计，呈现上下文信息优于规定程序工作流程。所有代码、数据和日志均可在 https://github.com/pepealonso95/TDAD 上公开获取。

</details>

---

## 2. CodeScout: An Effective Recipe for Reinforcement Learning of Code Search Agents / CodeScout：代码搜索代理强化学习的有效方法

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.17829v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17829v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17829v1)

**Categories**: cs.SE, cs.AI, cs.CL

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

A prerequisite for coding agents to perform tasks on large repositories is code localization - the identification of relevant files, classes, and functions to work on. While repository-level code localization has been performed using embedding-based retrieval approaches such as vector search, recent work has focused on developing agents to localize relevant code either as a standalone precursor to or interleaved with performing actual work. Most prior methods on agentic code search equip the agent with complex, specialized tools, such as repository graphs derived from static analysis. In this paper, we demonstrate that, with an effective reinforcement learning recipe, a coding agent equipped with nothing more than a standard Unix terminal can be trained to achieve strong results. Our experiments on three benchmarks (SWE-Bench Verified, Pro, and Lite) reveal that our models consistently achieve superior or competitive performance over 2-18x larger base and post-trained LLMs and sometimes approach performance provided by closed models like Claude Sonnet, even when using specialized scaffolds. Our work particularly focuses on techniques for re-purposing existing coding agent environments for code search, reward design, and RL optimization. We release the resulting model family, CodeScout, along with all our code and data for the community to build upon.

编码代理在大型存储库上执行任务的先决条件是代码本地化 - 识别要处理的相关文件、类和函数。虽然存储库级代码本地化是使用基于嵌入的检索方法（例如矢量搜索）来执行的，但最近的工作重点是开发代理来本地化相关代码，作为执行实际工作的独立前体或与执行实际工作交错。大多数先前的代理代码搜索方法都为代理配备了复杂的专用工具，例如从静态分析得出的存储库图。在本文中，我们证明，通过有效的强化学习方法，只需训练一个仅配备标准 Unix 终端的编码代理即可获得强大的结果。我们在三个基准（SWE-Bench Verified、Pro 和 Lite）上进行的实验表明，我们的模型始终比 2-18 倍大的基础和经过训练的 LLM 实现了优越或有竞争力的性能，有时甚至接近 Claude Sonnet 等封闭模型提供的性能，即使使用专门的支架也是如此。我们的工作特别关注重新利用现有编码代理环境进行代码搜索、奖励设计和强化学习优化的技术。我们发布了最终的模型系列 CodeScout 以及我们所有的代码和数据，供社区构建。

</details>

---

## 3. Federated Distributional Reinforcement Learning with Distributional Critic Regularization / 具有分布式批评正则化的联合分布式强化学习

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.17820v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17820v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17820v1)

**Categories**: cs.LG

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Federated reinforcement learning typically aggregates value functions or policies by parameter averaging, which emphasizes expected return and can obscure statistical multimodality and tail behavior that matter in safety-critical settings. We formalize federated distributional reinforcement learning (FedDistRL), where clients parametrize quantile value function critics and federate these networks only. We also propose TR-FedDistRL, which builds a per client, risk-aware Wasserstein barycenter over a temporal buffer. This local barycenter provides a reference region to constrain the parameter averaged critic, ensuring necessary distributional information is not averaged out during the federation process. The distributional trust region is implemented as a shrink-squash step around this reference. Under fixed-policy evaluation, the feasibility map is nonexpansive and the update is contractive in a probe-set Wasserstein metric under evaluation. Experiments on a bandit, multi-agent gridworld, and continuous highway environment show reduced mean-smearing, improved safety proxies (catastrophe/accident rate), and lower critic/policy drift versus mean-oriented and non-federated baselines.

联合强化学习通常通过参数平均来聚合价值函数或策略，这强调预期回报，并可能掩盖在安全关键环境中重要的统计多模态和尾部行为。我们将联合分布式强化学习（FedDistRL）形式化，其中客户参数化分位数价值函数批评家并仅联合这些网络。我们还提出了 TR-FedDistRL，它在时间缓冲区上构建一个每个客户端、具有风险意识的 Wasserstein 重心。该局部重心提供了一个参考区域来约束参数平均批评家，确保必要的分布信息在联邦过程中不会被平均掉。分布式信任区域是作为围绕该参考的收缩挤压步骤来实现的。在固定策略评估下，可行性图是非扩展的，并且更新在评估下的探针集 Wasserstein 度量中是收缩的。在强盗、多智能体网格世界和连续高速公路环境上进行的实验表明，与面向均值和非联合的基线相比，均值涂抹减少，安全代理（灾难/事故率）得到改善，批评家/策略漂移更低。

</details>

---

## 4. Governed Memory: A Production Architecture for Multi-Agent Workflows / 受控内存：多代理工作流的生产架构

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.17787v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17787v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17787v1)

**Categories**: cs.AI, cs.CL, cs.MA

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Enterprise AI deploys dozens of autonomous agent nodes across workflows, each acting on the same entities with no shared memory and no common governance. We identify five structural challenges arising from this memory governance gap: memory silos across agent workflows; governance fragmentation across teams and tools; unstructured memories unusable by downstream systems; redundant context delivery in autonomous multi-step executions; and silent quality degradation without feedback loops. We present Governed Memory, a shared memory and governance layer addressing this gap through four mechanisms: a dual memory model combining open-set atomic facts with schema-enforced typed properties; tiered governance routing with progressive context delivery; reflection-bounded retrieval with entity-scoped isolation; and a closed-loop schema lifecycle with AI-assisted authoring and automated per-property refinement. We validate each mechanism through controlled experiments (N=250, five content types): 99.6% fact recall with complementary dual-modality coverage; 92% governance routing precision; 50% token reduction from progressive delivery; zero cross-entity leakage across 500 adversarial queries; 100% adversarial governance compliance; and output quality saturation at approximately seven governed memories per entity. On the LoCoMo benchmark, the architecture achieves 74.8% overall accuracy, confirming that governance and schema enforcement impose no retrieval quality penalty. The system is in production at Personize.ai.

企业人工智能在工作流程中部署了数十个自主代理节点，每个节点都作用于相同的实体，没有共享内存，也没有共同的治理。我们确定了这种内存治理差距带来的五个结构性挑战：跨代理工作流程的内存孤岛；跨团队和工具的治理碎片化；下游系统无法使用非结构化内存；自主多步执行中的冗余上下文传递；以及无反馈循环的无声质量下降。我们提出了治理内存（Governed Memory），这是一个共享内存和治理层，通过四种机制解决了这一差距：将开放集原子事实与模式强制类型化属性相结合的双内存模型；具有渐进式上下文交付的分层治理路由；具有实体范围隔离的反射限制检索；以及具有人工智能辅助创作和自动每个属性细化的闭环模式生命周期。我们通过对照实验（N=250，五种内容类型）验证每种机制：99.6% 的事实召回率和互补的双模态覆盖率； 92%治理路由精度；渐进式交付减少 50% 代币； 500 个对抗性查询的跨实体零泄漏； 100%对抗式治理合规性；每个实体大约有七个受控存储器的输出质量饱和度。在 LoCoMo 基准测试中，该架构实现了 74.8% 的整体准确率，证实治理和模式实施不会造成检索质量损失。该系统正在 Personize.ai 投入生产。

</details>

---

## 5. MALLES: A Multi-agent LLMs-based Economic Sandbox with Consumer Preference Alignment / MALLES：基于法学硕士的多代理经济沙箱，与消费者偏好保持一致

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.17694v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17694v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17694v1)

**Categories**: cs.AI

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

In the real economy, modern decision-making is fundamentally challenged by high-dimensional, multimodal environments, which are further complicated by agent heterogeneity and combinatorial data sparsity. This paper introduces a Multi-Agent Large Language Model-based Economic Sandbox (MALLES), leveraging the inherent generalization capabilities of large-sacle models to establish a unified simulation framework applicable to cross-domain and cross-category scenarios. Central to our approach is a preference learning paradigm in which LLMs are economically aligned via post-training on extensive, heterogeneous transaction records across diverse product categories. This methodology enables the models to internalize and transfer latent consumer preference patterns, thereby mitigating the data sparsity issues prevalent in individual categories. To enhance simulation stability, we implement a mean-field mechanism designed to model the dynamic interactions between the product environment and customer populations, effectively stabilizing sampling processes within high-dimensional decision spaces. Furthermore, we propose a multi-agent discussion framework wherein specialized agents collaboratively process extensive product information. This architecture distributes cognitive load to alleviate single-agent attention bottlenecks and captures critical decision factors through structured dialogue. Experiments demonstrate that our framework achieves significant improvements in product selection accuracy, purchase quantity prediction, and simulation stability compared to existing economic and financial LLM simulation baselines. Our results substantiate the potential of large language models as a foundational pillar for high-fidelity, scalable decision simulation and latter analysis in the real economy based on foundational database.

在实体经济中，现代决策从根本上受到高维、多模态环境的挑战，而主体的异质性和组合数据的稀疏性使这些环境变得更加复杂。本文提出了一种基于多智能体大型语言模型的经济沙箱（MALLES），利用大型模型固有的泛化能力，建立适用于跨领域、跨品类场景的统一模拟框架。我们方法的核心是偏好学习范式，其中法学硕士通过对不同产品类别的广泛、异构交易记录进行培训后在经济上保持一致。这种方法使模型能够内化和转移潜在的消费者偏好模式，从而缓解各个类别中普遍存在的数据稀疏问题。为了增强模拟稳定性，我们实施了平均场机制，旨在对产品环境和客户群体之间的动态交互进行建模，从而有效稳定高维决策空间内的采样过程。此外，我们提出了一个多代理讨论框架，其中专门的代理协作处理广泛的产品信息。该架构分配认知负载以缓解单代理注意力瓶颈，并通过结构化对话捕获关键决策因素。实验表明，与现有的经济和金融 LLM 模拟基线相比，我们的框架在产品选择准确性、购买数量预测和模拟稳定性方面取得了显着提高。我们的结果证实了大型语言模型作为基于基础数据库的实体经济中高保真、可扩展的决策模拟和后期分析的基础支柱的潜力。

</details>

---

## 6. Can Blindfolded LLMs Still Trade? An Anonymization-First Framework for Portfolio Optimization / 蒙住眼睛的法学硕士还能进行交易吗？投资组合优化的匿名化优先框架

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.17692v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17692v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17692v1)

**Categories**: cs.LG, cs.AI, q-fin.CP, q-fin.PM

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

For LLM trading agents to be genuinely trustworthy, they must demonstrate understanding of market dynamics rather than exploitation of memorized ticker associations. Building responsible multi-agent systems demands rigorous signal validation: proving that predictions reflect legitimate patterns, not pre-trained recall. We address two sources of spurious performance: memorization bias from ticker-specific pre-training, and survivorship bias from flawed backtesting. Our approach is to blindfold the agents--anonymizing all identifiers--and verify whether meaningful signals persist. BlindTrade anonymizes tickers and company names, and four LLM agents output scores along with reasoning. We construct a GNN graph from reasoning embeddings and trade using PPO-DSR policy. On 2025 YTD (through 2025-08-01), we achieved Sharpe 1.40 +/- 0.22 across 20 seeds and validated signal legitimacy through negative control experiments. To assess robustness beyond a single OOS window, we additionally evaluate an extended period (2024--2025), revealing market-regime dependency: the policy excels in volatile conditions but shows reduced alpha in trending bull markets.

为了使法学硕士交易代理人真正值得信赖，他们必须表现出对市场动态的理解，而不是利用记忆的股票关联。构建负责任的多智能体系统需要严格的信号验证：证明预测反映了合法模式，而不是预先训练的回忆。我们解决了虚假表现的两个来源：特定股票预训练带来的记忆偏差，以及有缺陷的回测带来的生存偏差。我们的方法是蒙住代理的眼睛——匿名所有标识符——并验证有意义的信号是否持续存在。 BlindTrade 对股票和公司名称进行匿名处理，四名 LLM 代理输出分数以及推理。我们使用 PPO-DSR 策略从推理嵌入和交易构建 GNN 图。截至 2025 年年初（截至 2025 年 8 月 1 日），我们在 20 个种子中实现了 Sharpe 1.40 +/- 0.22，并通过阴性对照实验验证了信号的合法性。为了评估单个 OOS 窗口之外的稳健性，我们还评估了一个较长的时期（2024--2025），揭示了市场制度的依赖性：该政策在波动的条件下表现出色，但在趋势牛市中显示出阿尔法值降低。

</details>

---

## 7. AgentVLN: Towards Agentic Vision-and-Language Navigation / AgentVLN：迈向代理视觉和语言导航

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.17670v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17670v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17670v1)

**Categories**: cs.RO

**Code**: https://github.com/Allenxinn/AgentVLN.

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Vision-and-Language Navigation (VLN) requires an embodied agent to ground complex natural-language instructions into long-horizon navigation in unseen environments. While Vision-Language Models (VLMs) offer strong 2D semantic understanding, current VLN systems remain constrained by limited spatial perception, 2D-3D representation mismatch, and monocular scale ambiguity. In this paper, we propose AgentVLN, a novel and efficient embodied navigation framework that can be deployed on edge computing platforms. We formulate VLN as a Partially Observable Semi-Markov Decision Process (POSMDP) and introduce a VLM-as-Brain paradigm that decouples high-level semantic reasoning from perception and planning via a plug-and-play skill library. To resolve multi-level representation inconsistency, we design a cross-space representation mapping that projects perception-layer 3D topological waypoints into the image plane, yielding pixel-aligned visual prompts for the VLM. Building on this bridge, we integrate a context-aware self-correction and active exploration strategy to recover from occlusions and suppress error accumulation over long trajectories. To further address the spatial ambiguity of instructions in unstructured environments, we propose a Query-Driven Perceptual Chain-of-Thought (QD-PCoT) scheme, enabling the agent with the metacognitive ability to actively seek geometric depth information. Finally, we construct AgentVLN-Instruct, a large-scale instruction-tuning dataset with dynamic stage routing conditioned on target visibility. Extensive experiments show that AgentVLN consistently outperforms prior state-of-the-art methods (SOTA) on long-horizon VLN benchmarks, offering a practical paradigm for lightweight deployment of next-generation embodied navigation models. Code: https://github.com/Allenxinn/AgentVLN.

视觉和语言导航（VLN）需要一个实体代理将复杂的自然语言指令转化为看不见的环境中的长视野导航。虽然视觉语言模型 (VLM) 提供强大的 2D 语义理解，但当前的 VLN 系统仍然受到有限的空间感知、2D-3D 表示不匹配和单目尺度模糊性的限制。在本文中，我们提出了 AgentVLN，这是一种新颖且高效的嵌入式导航框架，可以部署在边缘计算平台上。我们将 VLN 制定为部分可观察半马尔可夫决策过程 (POSMDP)，并引入 VLM-as-Brain 范式，通过即插即用技能库将高级语义推理与感知和规划分离。为了解决多级表示不一致的问题，我们设计了一种跨空间表示映射，将感知层 3D 拓扑路径点投影到图像平面中，为 VLM 生成像素对齐的视觉提示。在此桥梁的基础上，我们集成了上下文感知自我校正和主动探索策略，以从遮挡中恢复并抑制长轨迹上的错误累积。为了进一步解决非结构化环境中指令的空间模糊性，我们提出了一种查询驱动的感知思维链（QD-PCoT）方案，使具有元认知能力的代理能够主动寻求几何深度信息。最后，我们构建了 AgentVLN-Instruct，这是一个大规模指令调整数据集，具有以目标可见性为条件的动态阶段路由。大量实验表明，AgentVLN 在长视野 VLN 基准上始终优于先前最先进的方法 (SOTA)，为下一代体现导航模型的轻量级部署提供了实用范例。代码：https://github.com/Allenxinn/AgentVLN。

</details>

---

## 8. VeriGrey: Greybox Agent Validation / VeriGrey：灰盒代理验证

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.17639v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17639v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17639v1)

**Categories**: cs.AI

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Agentic AI has been a topic of great interest recently. A Large Language Model (LLM) agent involves one or more LLMs in the back-end. In the front end, it conducts autonomous decision-making by combining the LLM outputs with results obtained by invoking several external tools. The autonomous interactions with the external environment introduce critical security risks.   In this paper, we present a grey-box approach to explore diverse behaviors and uncover security risks in LLM agents. Our approach VeriGrey uses the sequence of tools invoked as a feedback function to drive the testing process. This helps uncover infrequent but dangerous tool invocations that cause unexpected agent behavior. As mutation operators in the testing process, we mutate prompts to design pernicious injection prompts. This is carefully accomplished by linking the task of the agent to an injection task, so that the injection task becomes a necessary step of completing the agent functionality. Comparing our approach with a black-box baseline on the well-known AgentDojo benchmark, VeriGrey achieves 33% additional efficacy in finding indirect prompt injection vulnerabilities with a GPT-4.1 back-end.   We also conduct real-world case studies with the widely used coding agent Gemini CLI, and the well-known OpenClaw personal assistant. VeriGrey finds prompts inducing several attack scenarios that could not be identified by black-box approaches. In OpenClaw, by constructing a conversation agent which employs mutational fuzz testing as needed, VeriGrey is able to discover malicious skill variants from 10 malicious skills (with 10/10= 100% success rate on the Kimi-K2.5 LLM backend, and 9/10= 90% success rate on Opus 4.6 LLM backend). This demonstrates the value of a dynamic approach like VeriGrey to test agents, and to eventually lead to an agent assurance framework.

Agentic AI 最近成为人们非常感兴趣的话题。大型语言模型 (LLM) 代理涉及后端的一个或多个 LLM。在前端，它通过将LLM输出与调用多个外部工具获得的结果相结合来进行自主决策。与外部环境的自主交互会带来严重的安全风险。   在本文中，我们提出了一种灰盒方法来探索 LLM 代理中的不同行为并发现安全风险。我们的方法 VeriGrey 使用作为反馈功能调用的工具序列来驱动测试过程。这有助于发现导致意外代理行为的不常见但危险的工具调用。作为测试过程中的突变操作者，我们对提示进行突变以设计有害的注入提示。这是通过将代理的任务链接到注入任务来小心地完成的，以便注入任务成为完成代理功能的必要步骤。将我们的方法与著名的 AgentDojo 基准上的黑盒基线进行比较，VeriGrey 在使用 GPT-4.1 后端查找间接提示注入漏洞方面实现了 33% 的额外效率。   我们还使用广泛使用的编码代理 Gemini CLI 和著名的 OpenClaw 个人助理进行实际案例研究。 VeriGrey 发现提示会引发多种黑盒方法无法识别的攻击场景。在 OpenClaw 中，通过构建一个根据需要采用变异模糊测试的对话代理，VeriGrey 能够从 10 种恶意技能中发现恶意技能变体（在 Kimi-K2.5 LLM 后端上成功率为 10/10= 100%，在 Opus 4.6 LLM 后端上为 9/10= 90% 成功率）。这证明了像 VeriGrey 这样的动态方法对于测试代理并最终形成代理保证框架的价值。

</details>

---

## 9. Complementary Reinforcement Learning / 补充强化学习

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.17621v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17621v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17621v1)

**Categories**: cs.LG, cs.CL

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Reinforcement Learning (RL) has emerged as a powerful paradigm for training LLM-based agents, yet remains limited by low sample efficiency, stemming not only from sparse outcome feedback but also from the agent's inability to leverage prior experience across episodes. While augmenting agents with historical experience offers a promising remedy, existing approaches suffer from a critical weakness: the experience distilled from history is either stored statically or fail to coevolve with the improving actor, causing a progressive misalignment between the experience and the actor's evolving capability that diminishes its utility over the course of training. Inspired by complementary learning systems in neuroscience, we present Complementary RL to achieve seamless co-evolution of an experience extractor and a policy actor within the RL optimization loop. Specifically, the actor is optimized via sparse outcome-based rewards, while the experience extractor is optimized according to whether its distilled experiences demonstrably contribute to the actor's success, thereby evolving its experience management strategy in lockstep with the actor's growing capabilities. Empirically, Complementary RL outperforms outcome-based agentic RL baselines that do not learn from experience, achieving 10% performance improvement in single-task scenarios and exhibits robust scalability in multi-task settings. These results establish Complementary RL as a paradigm for efficient experience-driven agent learning.

强化学习 (RL) 已成为训练基于 LLM 的智能体的强大范例，但仍然受到样本效率低下的限制，这不仅源于稀疏的结果反馈，还源于智能体无法利用跨事件的先前经验。虽然用历史经验增强智能体提供了一种有希望的补救措施，但现有的方法存在一个严重的弱点：从历史中提取的经验要么静态存储，要么无法与改进的参与者共同进化，从而导致经验与参与者不断发展的能力之间逐渐错位，从而降低了其在训练过程中的效用。受到神经科学中互补学习系统的启发，我们提出了互补强化学习，以在强化学习优化循环中实现经验提取器和策略参与者的无缝协同进化。具体来说，参与者通过稀疏的基于结果的奖励进行优化，而经验提取器根据其提炼的经验是否明显有助于参与者的成功进行优化，从而与参与者不断增长的能力同步发展其经验管理策略。根据经验，互补强化学习优于不从经验中学习的基于结果的代理强化学习基线，在单任务场景中实现了 10% 的性能提升，并在多任务设置中表现出强大的可扩展性。这些结果将互补强化学习确立为高效的经验驱动代理学习的范例。

</details>

---

## 10. VeriAgent: A Tool-Integrated Multi-Agent System with Evolving Memory for PPA-Aware RTL Code Generation / VeriAgent：工具集成的多代理系统，具有不断发展的内存，用于生成 PPA 感知的 RTL 代码

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.17613v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17613v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17613v1)

**Categories**: cs.CL, cs.PL

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

LLMs have recently demonstrated strong capabilities in automatic RTL code generation, achieving high syntactic and functional correctness. However, most methods focus on functional correctness while overlooking critical physical design objectives, including Power, Performance, and Area. In this work, we propose a PPA-aware, tool-integrated multi-agent framework for high-quality verilog code generation. Our framework explicitly incorporates EDA tools into a closed-loop workflow composed of a \textit{Programmer Agent}, a \textit{Correctness Agent}, and a \textit{PPA Agent}, enabling joint optimization of functional correctness and physical metrics. To support continuous improvement without model retraining, we introduce an \textit{Evolved Memory Mechanism} that externalizes optimization experience into structured memory nodes. A dedicated memory manager dynamically maintains the memory pool and allows the system to refine strategies based on historical execution trajectories. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach achieves strong functional correctness while delivering significant improvements in PPA metrics. By integrating tool-driven feedback with structured and evolvable memory, our framework transforms RTL generation from one-shot reasoning into a continual, feedback-driven optimization process, providing a scalable pathway for deploying LLMs in real-world hardware design flows.

法学硕士最近展示了自动 RTL 代码生成的强大能力，实现了较高的语法和功能正确性。然而，大多数方法都注重功能正确性，而忽略了关键的物理设计目标，包括功耗、性能和面积。在这项工作中，我们提出了一个 PPA 感知、工具集成的多代理框架，用于生成高质量的 verilog 代码。我们的框架明确地将 EDA 工具合并到由 \textit{Programmer Agent}、\textit{Correctness Agent} 和 \textit{PPA Agent} 组成的闭环工作流程中，从而实现功能正确性和物理指标的联合优化。为了支持持续改进而无需模型重新训练，我们引入了 \textit{进化记忆机制}，将优化经验外化到结构化记忆节点中。专用内存管理器动态维护内存池，并允许系统根据历史执行轨迹完善策略。大量实验表明，我们的方法实现了强大的功能正确性，同时显着改进了 PPA 指标。通过将工具驱动的反馈与结构化和可进化的内存相集成，我们的框架将 RTL 生成从一次性推理转变为持续的、反馈驱动的优化过程，为在现实世界的硬件设计流程中部署 LLM 提供了一种可扩展的途径。

</details>

---

## 11. When Only the Final Text Survives: Implicit Execution Tracing for Multi-Agent Attribution / 当只有最终文本幸存时：多代理归因的隐式执行跟踪

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.17445v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17445v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17445v1)

**Categories**: cs.AI, cs.CL

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

When a multi-agent system produces an incorrect or harmful answer, who is accountable if execution logs and agent identifiers are unavailable? Multi-agent language systems increasingly rely on structured interactions such as delegation and iterative refinement, yet the final output often obscures the underlying interaction topology and agent contributions. We introduce IET (Implicit Execution Tracing), a metadata-independent framework that enables token-level attribution directly from generated text and a simple mechanism for interaction topology reconstruction. During generation, agent-specific keyed signals are embedded into the token distribution, transforming the text into a self-describing execution trace detectable only with a secret key. At detection time, a transition-aware scoring method identifies agent handover points and reconstructs the interaction graph. Experiments show that IET recovers agent segments and coordination structure with high accuracy while preserving generation quality, enabling privacy-preserving auditing for multi-agent language systems.

当多代理系统产生不正确或有害的答案时，如果执行日志和代理标识符不可用，谁负责？多智能体语言系统越来越依赖于结构化交互，例如委托和迭代细化，但最终的输出往往掩盖了底层交互拓扑和智能体贡献。我们引入了 IET（隐式执行跟踪），这是一个独立于元数据的框架，可以直接从生成的文本中进行令牌级归因，以及用于交互拓扑重建的简单机制。在生成过程中，特定于代理的键控信号被嵌入到令牌分布中，将文本转换为只能使用密钥检测到的自描述执行跟踪。在检测时，转换感知评分方法识别代理切换点并重建交互图。实验表明，IET 可以高精度地恢复智能体片段和协调结构，同时保持生成质量，从而实现多智能体语言系统的隐私保护审计。

</details>

---

## 12. From Digital Twins to World Models:Opportunities, Challenges, and Applications for Mobile Edge General Intelligence / 从数字孪生到世界模型：移动边缘通用智能的机遇、挑战和应用

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.17420v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17420v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17420v1)

**Categories**: cs.AI

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

The rapid evolution toward 6G and beyond communication systems is accelerating the convergence of digital twins and world models at the network edge. Traditional digital twins provide high-fidelity representations of physical systems and support monitoring, analysis, and offline optimization. However, in highly dynamic edge environments, they face limitations in autonomy, adaptability, and scalability. This paper presents a systematic survey of the transition from digital twins to world models and discusses its role in enabling edge general intelligence (EGI). First, the paper clarifies the conceptual differences between digital twins and world models and highlights the shift from physics-based, centralized, and system-centric replicas to data-driven, decentralized, and agent-centric internal models. This discussion helps readers gain a clear understanding of how this transition enables more adaptive, autonomous, and resource-efficient intelligence at the network edge. The paper reviews the design principles, architectures, and key components of world models, including perception, latent state representation, dynamics learning, imagination-based planning, and memory. In addition, it examines the integration of world models and digital twins in wireless EGI systems and surveys emerging applications in integrated sensing and communications, semantic communication, air-ground networks, and low-altitude wireless networks. Finally, this survey provides a systematic roadmap and practical insights for designing world-model-driven edge intelligence systems in wireless and edge computing environments. It also outlines key research challenges and future directions toward scalable, reliable, and interoperable world models for edge-native agentic AI.

向 6G 及其他通信系统的快速发展正在加速数字孪生和世界模型在网络边缘的融合。传统的数字孪生提供物理系统的高保真表示，并支持监控、分析和离线优化。然而，在高度动态的边缘环境中，它们在自主性、适应性和可扩展性方面面临限制。本文对从数字孪生到世界模型的转变进行了系统调查，并讨论了其在实现边缘通用智能 (EGI) 方面的作用。首先，本文阐明了数字孪生和世界模型之间的概念差异，并强调了从基于物理的、集中式的、以系统为中心的复制品到数据驱动的、去中心化的、以代理为中心的内部模型的转变。本讨论有助于读者清楚地了解这种转变如何在网络边缘实现更具适应性、自主性和资源效率的智能。本文回顾了世界模型的设计原则、架构和关键组成部分，包括感知、潜在状态表示、动态学习、基于想象力的规划和记忆。此外，它还研究了世界模型和数字孪生在无线 EGI 系统中的集成，并调查了集成传感和通信、语义通信、空地网络和低空无线网络中的新兴应用。最后，这项调查为在无线和边缘计算环境中设计世界模型驱动的边缘智能系统提供了系统的路线图和实用见解。它还概述了边缘原生代理人工智能的可扩展、可靠和可互操作的世界模型的关键研究挑战和未来方向。

</details>

---

## 13. Caging the Agents: A Zero Trust Security Architecture for Autonomous AI in Healthcare / 关押代理：医疗保健领域自主人工智能的零信任安全架构

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.17419v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17419v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17419v1)

**Categories**: cs.CR, cs.AI

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Autonomous AI agents powered by large language models are being deployed in production with capabilities including shell execution, file system access, database queries, and multi-party communication. Recent red teaming research demonstrates that these agents exhibit critical vulnerabilities in realistic settings: unauthorized compliance with non-owner instructions, sensitive information disclosure, identity spoofing, cross-agent propagation of unsafe practices, and indirect prompt injection through external resources [7]. In healthcare environments processing Protected Health Information, every such vulnerability becomes a potential HIPAA violation. This paper presents a security architecture deployed for nine autonomous AI agents in production at a healthcare technology company. We develop a six-domain threat model for agentic AI in healthcare covering credential exposure, execution capability abuse, network egress exfiltration, prompt integrity failures, database access risks, and fleet configuration drift. We implement four-layer defense in depth: (1) kernel level workload isolation using gVisor on Kubernetes, (2) credential proxy sidecars preventing agent containers from accessing raw secrets, (3) network egress policies restricting each agent to allowlisted destinations, and (4) a prompt integrity framework with structured metadata envelopes and untrusted content labeling. We report results from 90 days of deployment including four HIGH severity findings discovered and remediated by an automated security audit agent, progressive fleet hardening across three VM image generations, and defense coverage mapped to all eleven attack patterns from recent literature. All configurations, audit tooling, and the prompt integrity framework are released as open source.

由大型语言模型支持的自主人工智能代理正在生产中部署，其功能包括 shell 执行、文件系统访问、数据库查询和多方通信。最近的红队研究表明，这些代理在现实环境中表现出严重的漏洞：未经授权地遵守非所有者指令、敏感信息泄露、身份欺骗、不安全做法的跨代理传播以及通过外部资源的间接提示注入[7]。在处理受保护健康信息的医疗保健环境中，每个此类漏洞都可能违反 HIPAA。本文介绍了为医疗保健技术公司生产中的九个自主人工智能代理部署的安全架构。我们为医疗保健领域的代理人工智能开发了一个六域威胁模型，涵盖凭证暴露、执行能力滥用、网络出口渗透、提示完整性故障、数据库访问风险和车队配置漂移。我们深入实施四层防御：(1) 在 Kubernetes 上使用 gVisor 进行内核级工作负载隔离，(2) 凭证代理 sidecar 防止代理容器访问原始机密，(3) 网络出口策略将每个代理限制在允许列表中的目的地，以及 (4) 具有结构化元数据信封和不可信内容标签的即时完整性框架。我们报告了 90 天的部署结果，包括由自动安全审计代理发现并修复的四个高严重性问题、跨三个虚拟机映像代的渐进式队列强化，以及映射到最新文献中所有 11 种攻击模式的防御覆盖范围。所有配置、审核工具和提示完整性框架均作为开源发布。

</details>

---

## 14. ReLMXEL: Adaptive RL-Based Memory Controller with Explainable Energy and Latency Optimization / ReLMXEL：基于 RL 的自适应内存控制器，具有可解释的能量和延迟优化

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.17309v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17309v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17309v1)

**Categories**: cs.AR, cs.AI, cs.LG, cs.MA, eess.SY

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Reducing latency and energy consumption is critical to improving the efficiency of memory systems in modern computing. This work introduces ReLMXEL (Reinforcement Learning for Memory Controller with Explainable Energy and Latency Optimization), a explainable multi-agent online reinforcement learning framework that dynamically optimizes memory controller parameters using reward decomposition. ReLMXEL operates within the memory controller, leveraging detailed memory behavior metrics to guide decision-making. Experimental evaluations across diverse workloads demonstrate consistent performance gains over baseline configurations, with refinements driven by workload-specific memory access behaviour. By incorporating explainability into the learning process, ReLMXEL not only enhances performance but also increases the transparency of control decisions, paving the way for more accountable and adaptive memory system designs.

减少延迟和能耗对于提高现代计算中内存系统的效率至关重要。这项工作介绍了 ReLMXEL（具有可解释能量和延迟优化的内存控制器强化学习），这是一种可解释的多智能体在线强化学习框架，可使用奖励分解动态优化内存控制器参数。 ReLMXEL 在内存控制器内运行，利用详细的内存行为指标来指导决策。跨不同工作负载的实验评估表明，与基准配置相比，性能得到了一致的提升，并由特定工作负载的内存访问行为驱动进行了改进。通过将可解释性纳入学习过程，ReLMXEL 不仅提高了性能，还提高了控制决策的透明度，为更负责任和适应性更强的记忆系统设计铺平了道路。

</details>

---

## 15. Symphony: A Cognitively-Inspired Multi-Agent System for Long-Video Understanding / Symphony：用于长视频理解的认知启发多智能体系统

**Date**: 2026-03-18 | **arXiv**: [2603.17307v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17307v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17307v1)

**Categories**: cs.CV, cs.AI

**Code**: https://github.com/Haiyang0226/Symphony.

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Despite rapid developments and widespread applications of MLLM agents, they still struggle with long-form video understanding (LVU) tasks, which are characterized by high information density and extended temporal spans. Recent research on LVU agents demonstrates that simple task decomposition and collaboration mechanisms are insufficient for long-chain reasoning tasks. Moreover, directly reducing the time context through embedding-based retrieval may lose key information of complex problems. In this paper, we propose Symphony, a multi-agent system, to alleviate these limitations. By emulating human cognition patterns, Symphony decomposes LVU into fine-grained subtasks and incorporates a deep reasoning collaboration mechanism enhanced by reflection, effectively improving the reasoning capability. Additionally, Symphony provides a VLM-based grounding approach to analyze LVU tasks and assess the relevance of video segments, which significantly enhances the ability to locate complex problems with implicit intentions and large temporal spans. Experimental results show that Symphony achieves state-of-the-art performance on LVBench, LongVideoBench, VideoMME, and MLVU, with a 5.0% improvement over the prior state-of-the-art method on LVBench. Code is available at https://github.com/Haiyang0226/Symphony.

尽管 MLLM 代理得到了快速发展和广泛应用，但它们仍然难以应对长格式视频理解（LVU）任务，这些任务的特点是信息密度高和时间跨度长。最近对 LVU 智能体的研究表明，简单的任务分解和协作机制不足以完成长链推理任务。此外，通过基于嵌入的检索直接减少时间上下文可能会丢失复杂问题的关键信息。在本文中，我们提出了 Symphony，一个多代理系统，来缓解这些限制。 Symphony 通过模仿人类认知模式，将 LVU 分解为细粒度的子任务，并融入反射增强的深度推理协作机制，有效提高推理能力。此外，Symphony 还提供了基于 VLM 的基础方法来分析 LVU 任务并评估视频片段的相关性，这显着增强了定位具有隐式意图和大时间跨度的复杂问题的能力。实验结果表明，Symphony 在 LVBench、LongVideoBench、VideoMME 和 MLVU 上实现了 state-of-the-art 的性能，比之前在 LVBench 上的 state-of-the-art 方法提高了 5.0%。代码可在 https://github.com/Haiyang0226/Symphony 获取。

</details>

---

## 16. Anonymous-by-Construction: An LLM-Driven Framework for Privacy-Preserving Text / 构造匿名：法学硕士驱动的隐私保护文本框架

**Date**: 2026-03-17 | **arXiv**: [2603.17217v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17217v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17217v1)

**Categories**: cs.CL, cs.AI, cs.LG

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Responsible use of AI demands that we protect sensitive information without undermining the usefulness of data, an imperative that has become acute in the age of large language models. We address this challenge with an on-premise, LLM-driven substitution pipeline that anonymizes text by replacing personally identifiable information (PII) with realistic, type-consistent surrogates. Executed entirely within organizational boundaries using local LLMs, the approach prevents data egress while preserving fluency and task-relevant semantics.   We conduct a systematic, multi-metric, cross-technique evaluation on the Action-Based Conversation Dataset, benchmarking against industry standards (Microsoft Presidio and Google DLP) and a state-of-the-art approach (ZSTS, in redaction-only and redaction-plus-substitution variants). Our protocol jointly measures privacy, semantic utility, and trainability under privacy via a lifecycle-ready criterion obtained by fine-tuning a compact encoder (BERT+LoRA) on sanitized text. In addition, we assess agentic Q&A performance by inserting an on-premise anonymization layer before the answering LLM and evaluating the quality of its responses. This intermediate, type-preserving substitution stage ensures that no sensitive content is exposed to third-party APIs, enabling responsible deployment of Q\&A agents without compromising confidentiality.   Our method attains state-of-the-art privacy, minimal topical drift, strong factual utility, and low trainability loss, outperforming rule-based approaches and named-entity recognition (NER) baselines and ZSTS variants on the combined privacy--utility--trainability frontier. These results show that local LLM substitution yields anonymized corpora that are both responsible to use and operationally valuable: safe for agentic pipelines and suitable for downstream fine-tuning with limited degradation.

负责任地使用人工智能要求我们在不损害数据有用性的情况下保护敏感信息，这一点在大型语言模型时代变得越来越迫切。我们通过本地 LLM 驱动的替换管道来应对这一挑战，该管道通过用真实的、类型一致的代理替换个人身份信息 (PII) 来匿名化文本。该方法完全在组织边界内使用本地法学硕士执行，可防止数据流出，同时保持流畅性和任务相关语义。   我们对基于操作的对话数据集进行了系统性、多指标、跨技术的评估，以行业标准（Microsoft Presidio 和 Google DLP）和最先进的方法（ZSTS，仅编辑和编辑加替换变体）为基准。我们的协议通过在净化文本上微调紧凑编码器（BERT+LoRA）获得的生命周期就绪标准来联合测量隐私、语义效用和隐私下的可训练性。此外，我们通过在回答 LLM 之前插入本地匿名层并评估其响应的质量来评估代理问答性能。这个中间的、类型保留的替换阶段可确保敏感内容不会暴露给第三方 API，从而能够在不影响机密性的情况下负责任地部署问答代理。   我们的方法实现了最先进的隐私、最小的主题漂移、强大的事实实用性和低可训练性损失，在隐私-实用性-可训练性组合前沿上优于基于规则的方法和命名实体识别（NER）基线和 ZSTS 变体。这些结果表明，本地法学硕士替代产生了匿名语料库，该语料库既负责使用又具有操作价值：对于代理管道来说是安全的，并且适合下游微调，且降级有限。

</details>

---

## 17. AI Scientist via Synthetic Task Scaling / 人工智能科学家通过综合任务扩展

**Date**: 2026-03-17 | **arXiv**: [2603.17216v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17216v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17216v1)

**Categories**: cs.AI

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

With the advent of AI agents, automatic scientific discovery has become a tenable goal. Many recent works scaffold agentic systems that can perform machine learning research, but don't offer a principled way to train such agents -- and current LLMs often generate plausible-looking but ineffective ideas. To make progress on training agents that can learn from doing, we provide a novel synthetic environment generation pipeline targeting machine learning agents. Our pipeline automatically synthesizes machine learning challenges compatible with the SWE-agent framework, covering topic sampling, dataset proposal, and code generation. The resulting synthetic tasks are 1) grounded in real machine learning datasets, because the proposed datasets are verified against the Huggingface API and are 2) verified for higher quality with a self-debugging loop. To validate the effectiveness of our synthetic tasks, we tackle MLGym, a benchmark for machine learning tasks. From the synthetic tasks, we sample trajectories from a teacher model (GPT-5), then use the trajectories to train a student model (Qwen3-4B and Qwen3-8B). The student models trained with our synthetic tasks achieve improved performance on MLGym, raising the AUP metric by 9% for Qwen3-4B and 12% for Qwen3-8B.

随着人工智能代理的出现，自动科学发现已成为一个可行的目标。最近的许多工作都构建了可以执行机器学习研究的代理系统，但没有提供训练此类代理的原则性方法——而当前的法学硕士经常产生看似合理但无效的想法。为了在可以从实践中学习的训练代理方面取得进展，我们提供了一种针对机器学习代理的新颖的合成环境生成管道。我们的管道自动综合与 SWE-agent 框架兼容的机器学习挑战，涵盖主题采样、数据集提议和代码生成。由此产生的合成任务 1) 基于真实的机器学习数据集，因为所提出的数据集是针对 Huggingface API 进行验证的，并且 2) 通过自调试循环验证了更高的质量。为了验证我们的综合任务的有效性，我们使用了 MLGym，这是机器学习任务的基准。从综合任务中，我们从教师模型 (GPT-5) 中采样轨迹，然后使用轨迹来训练学生模型（Qwen3-4B 和 Qwen3-8B）。使用我们的综合任务训练的学生模型在 MLGym 上的性能得到了提高，Qwen3-4B 的 AUP 指标提高了 9%，Qwen3-8B 的 AUP 指标提高了 12%。

</details>

---

## 18. CODMAS: A Dialectic Multi-Agent Collaborative Framework for Structured RTL Optimization / CODMAS：用于结构化 RTL 优化的辩证多智能体协作框架

**Date**: 2026-03-17 | **arXiv**: [2603.17204v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17204v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17204v1)

**Categories**: cs.CL, cs.AR, cs.PL

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Optimizing Register Transfer Level (RTL) code is a critical step in Electronic Design Automation (EDA) for improving power, performance, and area (PPA). We present CODMAS (Collaborative Optimization via a Dialectic Multi-Agent System), a framework that combines structured dialectic reasoning with domain-aware code generation and deterministic evaluation to automate RTL optimization. At the core of CODMAS are two dialectic agents: the Articulator, inspired by rubber-duck debugging, which articulates stepwise transformation plans and exposes latent assumptions; and the Hypothesis Partner, which predicts outcomes and reconciles deviations between expected and actual behavior to guide targeted refinements. These agents direct a Domain-Specific Coding Agent (DCA) to generate architecture-aware Verilog edits and a Code Evaluation Agent (CEA) to verify syntax, functionality, and PPA metrics. We introduce RTLOPT, a benchmark of 120 Verilog triples (unoptimized, optimized, testbench) for pipelining and clock-gating transformations. Across proprietary and open LLMs, CODMAS achieves ~25% reduction in critical path delay for pipelining and ~22% power reduction for clock gating, while reducing functional and compilation failures compared to strong prompting and agentic baselines. These results demonstrate that structured multi-agent reasoning can significantly enhance automated RTL optimization and scale to more complex designs and broader optimization tasks.

优化寄存器传输级 (RTL) 代码是电子设计自动化 (EDA) 中提高功耗、性能和面积 (PPA) 的关键步骤。我们提出了 CODMAS（通过辩证多代理系统进行协作优化），这是一个框架，它将结构化辩证推理与领域感知代码生成和确定性评估相结合，以自动实现 RTL 优化。 CODMAS 的核心是两个辩证代理：Articulator，受橡皮鸭调试启发，阐明逐步转换计划并揭示潜在假设；假设合作伙伴，预测结果并协调预期行为和实际行为之间的偏差，以指导有针对性的改进。这些代理指导特定领域编码代理 (DCA) 生成架构感知的 Verilog 编辑，并指导代码评估代理 (CEA) 验证语法、功能和 PPA 指标。我们引入 RTLOPT，这是一个用于流水线和时钟门控转换的 120 个 Verilog 三元组（未优化、优化、测试平台）的基准。在专有和开放的法学硕士中，与强提示和代理基线相比，CODMAS 使流水线的关键路径延迟减少了约 25%，时钟门控的功耗减少了约 22%，同时减少了功能和编译失败。这些结果表明，结构化多智能体推理可以显着增强自动化 RTL 优化，并扩展到更复杂的设计和更广泛的优化任务。

</details>

---

## 19. Tabular LLMs for Interpretable Few-Shot Alzheimer's Disease Prediction with Multimodal Biomedical Data / 利用多模态生物医学数据进行可解释的小样本阿尔茨海默病预测的表格法学硕士

**Date**: 2026-03-17 | **arXiv**: [2603.17191v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17191v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17191v1)

**Categories**: cs.CL, cs.LG, q-bio.QM

**Code**: https://github.com/sophie-kearney/TAP-GPT.

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Accurate diagnosis of Alzheimer's disease (AD) requires handling tabular biomarker data, yet such data are often small and incomplete, where deep learning models frequently fail to outperform classical methods. Pretrained large language models (LLMs) offer few-shot generalization, structured reasoning, and interpretable outputs, providing a powerful paradigm shift for clinical prediction. We propose TAP-GPT Tabular Alzheimer's Prediction GPT, a domain-adapted tabular LLM framework built on TableGPT2 and fine-tuned for few-shot AD classification using tabular prompts rather than plain texts. We evaluate TAP-GPT across four ADNI-derived datasets, including QT-PAD biomarkers and region-level structural MRI, amyloid PET, and tau PET for binary AD classification. Across multimodal and unimodal settings, TAP-GPT improves upon its backbone models and outperforms traditional machine learning baselines in the few-shot setting while remaining competitive with state-of-the-art general-purpose LLMs. We show that feature selection mitigates degradation in high-dimensional inputs and that TAP-GPT maintains stable performance under simulated and real-world missingness without imputation. Additionally, TAP-GPT produces structured, modality-aware reasoning aligned with established AD biology and shows greater stability under self-reflection, supporting its use in iterative multi-agent systems. To our knowledge, this is the first systematic application of a tabular-specialized LLM to multimodal biomarker-based AD prediction, demonstrating that such pretrained models can effectively address structured clinical prediction tasks and laying the foundation for tabular LLM-driven multi-agent clinical decision-support systems. The source code is publicly available on GitHub: https://github.com/sophie-kearney/TAP-GPT.

阿尔茨海默病 (AD) 的准确诊断需要处理表格生物标志物数据，但此类数据通常很小且不完整，深度学习模型常常无法超越经典方法。预训练的大语言模型 (LLM) 提供少样本泛化、结构化推理和可解释的输出，为临床预测提供强大的范式转变。我们提出了 TAP-GPT 表格式阿尔茨海默病预测 GPT，这是一个基于 TableGPT2 构建的领域适应表格式 LLM 框架，并使用表格提示而不是纯文本进行了少量 AD 分类的微调。我们在四个 ADNI 衍生数据集上评估 TAP-GPT，包括 QT-PAD 生物标志物和区域级结构 MRI、淀粉样蛋白 PET 和用于二元 AD 分类的 tau PET。在多模态和单模态设置中，TAP-GPT 改进了其主干模型，并在少数样本设置中超越了传统的机器学习基线，同时保持了与最先进的通用法学硕士的竞争力。我们表明，特征选择可以减轻高维输入的退化，并且 TAP-GPT 在模拟和现实世界缺失情况下保持稳定的性能而无需插补。此外，TAP-GPT 产生与已建立的 AD 生物学一致的结构化、模态感知推理，并在自我反思下表现出更大的稳定性，支持其在迭代多智能体系统中的使用。据我们所知，这是表格专业法学硕士在基于多模态生物标志物的AD预测中的首次系统应用，证明这种预训练模型可以有效地解决结构化临床预测任务，并为表格法学硕士驱动的多智能体临床决策支持系统奠定基础。源代码可在 GitHub 上公开获取：https://github.com/sophie-kearney/TAP-GPT。

</details>

---

## 20. PAuth - Precise Task-Scoped Authorization For Agents / PAuth - 针对代理的精确任务范围授权

**Date**: 2026-03-17 | **arXiv**: [2603.17170v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17170v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17170v1)

**Categories**: cs.CR, cs.AI, cs.PL

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

The emerging agentic web envisions AI agents that reliably fulfill users' natural-language (NL)-based tasks by interacting with existing web services. However, existing authorization models are misaligned with this vision. In particular, today's operator-scoped authorization, exemplified by OAuth, grants broad permissions tied to operators (e.g., the transfer operator) rather than to the specific operations (e.g., transfer $100 to Bob) implied by a user's task. This will inevitably result in overprivileged agents.   We introduce Precise Task-Scoped Implicit Authorization (PAuth), a fundamentally different model in which submitting an NL task implicitly authorizes only the concrete operations required for its faithful execution. To make this enforceable at servers, we propose NL slices: symbolic specifications of the calls each service expects, derived from the task and upstream results. Complementing this, we also propose envelopes: special data structure to bind each operand's concrete value to its symbolic provenance, enabling servers to verify that all operands arise from legitimate computations.   PAuth is prototyped in the agent-security evaluation framework AgentDojo. We evaluate it in both benign settings and attack scenarios where a spurious operation is injected into an otherwise normal task. In all benign tests, PAuth executes the tasks successfully without requiring any additional permissions. In all attack tests, PAuth correctly raises warnings about missing permissions. These results demonstrate that PAuth's reasoning about permissions is indeed precise. We further analyze the characteristics of these tasks and measure the associated token costs.

新兴的代理网络设想人工智能代理能够通过与现有网络服务交互来可靠地完成用户基于自然语言（NL）的任务。然而，现有的授权模式与这一愿景不一致。特别是，今天的操作员范围授权（以 OAuth 为例）授予与操作员（例如，转账操作员）相关的广泛权限，而不是与用户任务所暗示的特定操作（例如，向 Bob 转账 100 美元）相关的权限。这将不可避免地导致代理人特权过高。   我们引入了精确任务范围隐式授权 (PAuth)，这是一种完全不同的模型，其中提交 NL 任务隐式仅授权其忠实执行所需的具体操作。为了使这一点在服务器上可执行，我们提出了 NL 切片：每个服务期望的调用的符号规范，源自任务和上游结果。作为补充，我们还提出了信封：将每个操作数的具体值与其符号来源绑定的特殊数据结构，使服务器能够验证所有操作数是否来自合法计算。   PAuth 的原型是在代理安全评估框架 AgentDojo 中。我们在良性设置和攻击场景中对其进行评估，其中将虚假操作注入到其他正常任务中。在所有良性测试中，PAuth 无需任何额外权限即可成功执行任务。在所有攻击测试中，PAuth 正确地发出有关缺少权限的警告。这些结果表明 PAuth 关于权限的推理确实是准确的。我们进一步分析这些任务的特征并衡量相关的代币成本。

</details>

---

## 21. How Clued up are LLMs? Evaluating Multi-Step Deductive Reasoning in a Text-Based Game Environment / LLM 的情况如何？在基于文本的游戏环境中评估多步演绎推理

**Date**: 2026-03-17 | **arXiv**: [2603.17169v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17169v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17169v1)

**Categories**: cs.AI, cs.CL

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Deducing whodunit proves challenging for LLM agents. In this paper, we implement a text-based multi-agent version of the classic board game Clue as a rule-based testbed for evaluating multi-step deductive reasoning, with six agents drawn from GPT-4o-mini and Gemini-2.5-Flash. We further investigate whether fine-tuning on structured logic puzzles transfers to improved in-game reasoning and gameplay. Across 18 simulated games, agents achieve only four correct wins, indicating difficulty in maintaining consistent deductive reasoning over the course of a full game. Additionally, we find that fine-tuning does not reliably improve performance and, in some cases, appears to increase reasoning volume without improving reasoning precision.

事实证明，推断侦探小说对于法学硕士代理人来说具有挑战性。在本文中，我们实现了经典棋盘游戏 Clue 的基于文本的多智能体版本，作为评估多步演绎推理的基于规则的测试平台，其中六个智能体来自 GPT-4o-mini 和 Gemini-2.5-Flash。我们进一步研究对结构化逻辑谜题的微调是否会改善游戏内的推理和游戏玩法。在 18 场模拟游戏中，智能体仅取得了 4 次正确的胜利，这表明在整个游戏过程中保持一致的演绎推理很困难。此外，我们发现微调并不能可靠地提高性能，并且在某些情况下，似乎会增加推理量而不会提高推理精度。

</details>

---

## 22. Intent Formalization: A Grand Challenge for Reliable Coding in the Age of AI Agents / 意图形式化：人工智能代理时代可靠编码的巨大挑战

**Date**: 2026-03-17 | **arXiv**: [2603.17150v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17150v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17150v1)

**Categories**: cs.SE, cs.AI, cs.PL

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Agentic AI systems can now generate code with remarkable fluency, but a fundamental question remains: \emph{does the generated code actually do what the user intended?} The gap between informal natural language requirements and precise program behavior -- the \emph{intent gap} -- has always plagued software engineering, but AI-generated code amplifies it to an unprecedented scale. This article argues that \textbf{intent formalization} -- the translation of informal user intent into a set of checkable formal specifications -- is the key challenge that will determine whether AI makes software more reliable or merely more abundant. Intent formalization offers a tradeoff spectrum suitable to the reliability needs of different contexts: from lightweight tests that disambiguate likely misinterpretations, through full functional specifications for formal verification, to domain-specific languages from which correct code is synthesized automatically. The central bottleneck is \emph{validating specifications}: since there is no oracle for specification correctness other than the user, we need semi-automated metrics that can assess specification quality with or without code, through lightweight user interaction and proxy artifacts such as tests. We survey early research that demonstrates the \emph{potential} of this approach: interactive test-driven formalization that improves program correctness, AI-generated postconditions that catch real-world bugs missed by prior methods, and end-to-end verified pipelines that produce provably correct code from informal specifications. We outline the open research challenges -- scaling beyond benchmarks, achieving compositionality over changes, metrics for validating specifications, handling rich logics, designing human-AI specification interactions -- that define a research agenda spanning AI, programming languages, formal methods, and human-computer interaction.

代理人工智能系统现在可以非常流畅地生成代码，但仍然存在一个基本问题：\emph{生成的代码实际上是否按照用户的意图执行？}非正式的自然语言要求和精确的程序行为之间的差距 - \emph{意图差距} - 一直困扰着软件工程，但人工智能生成的代码将其放大到前所未有的规模。本文认为，\textbf{意图形式化}——将非正式的用户意图转化为一组可检查的正式规范——是决定人工智能是否使软件更可靠或仅仅更丰富的关键挑战。意图形式化提供了适合不同环境可靠性需求的权衡范围：从消除可能的误解的轻量级测试，到形式验证的完整功能规范，再到自动合成正确代码的领域特定语言。核心瓶颈是 \emph{验证规范}：由于除了用户之外没有其他预言机来保证规范的正确性，因此我们需要半自动化的指标，可以通过轻量级用户交互和测试等代理工件来评估有或没有代码的规范质量。我们调查了早期研究，证明了这种方法的潜力：交互式测试驱动的形式化可以提高程序的正确性，人工智能生成的后置条件可以捕获先前方法遗漏的现实世界错误，以及端到端验证的管道可以从非正式规范中生成可证明正确的代码。我们概述了开放研究挑战——超越基准、实现变化的组合性、验证规范的指标、处理丰富的逻辑、设计人机交互规范——定义了涵盖人工智能、编程语言、形式化方法和人机交互的研究议程。

</details>

---

## 23. Cascade-Aware Multi-Agent Routing: Spatio-Temporal Sidecars and Geometry-Switching / 级联感知多代理路由：时空 Sidecar 和几何切换

**Date**: 2026-03-17 | **arXiv**: [2603.17112v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17112v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17112v1)

**Categories**: cs.AI, cs.LG

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

A common architectural pattern in advanced AI reasoning systems is the symbolic graph network: specialized agents or modules connected by delegation edges, routing tasks through a dynamic execution graph. Current schedulers optimize load and fitness but are geometry-blind: they do not model how failures propagate differently in tree-like versus cyclic regimes. In tree-like delegation, a single failure can cascade exponentially; in dense cyclic graphs, failures tend to self-limit. We identify this observability gap, quantify its system-level cost, and propose a lightweight mitigation.   We formulate online geometry control for route-risk estimation on time-indexed execution graphs with route-local failure history. Our approach combines (i) a Euclidean spatio-temporal propagation baseline, (ii) a hyperbolic route-risk model with temporal decay (and optional burst excitation), and (iii) a learned geometry selector over structural features. The selector is a compact MLP (9->12->1) using six topology statistics plus three geometry-aware signals: BFS shell-growth slope, cycle-rank norm, and fitted Poincare curvature. On the Genesis 3 benchmark distribution, adaptive switching improves win rate in the hardest non_tree regime from 64-72% (fixed hyperbolic variants) to 92%, and achieves 87.2% overall win rate.   To measure total system value, we compare against Genesis 3 routing without any spatio-temporal sidecar, using only native bandit/LinUCB signals (team fitness and mean node load). This baseline achieves 50.4% win rate overall and 20% in tree-like regimes; the full sidecar recovers 87.2% overall (+36.8 pp), with +48 to +68 pp gains in tree-like settings, consistent with a cascade-sensitivity analysis. Overall, a 133-parameter sidecar substantially mitigates geometry-blind failure propagation in one high-capability execution-graph system.

高级人工智能推理系统中的常见架构模式是符号图网络：通过委托边连接的专用代理或模块，通过动态执行图路由任务。当前的调度程序优化了负载和适应度，但是是几何盲的：它们没有模拟故障在树状和循环状态下如何不同地传播。在树状委托中，单个故障可能会呈指数级联；在密集循环图中，故障往往会自我限制。我们确定了这种可观察性差距，量化了其系统级成本，并提出了轻量级的缓解措施。   我们制定了在线几何控制，用于在时间索引执行图上估计路线风险，并具有路线局部故障历史记录。我们的方法结合了（i）欧几里德时空传播基线，（ii）具有时间衰减（和可选的突发激励）的双曲路线风险模型，以及（iii）结构特征上的学习几何选择器。选择器是一个紧凑的 MLP (9->12->1)，使用六个拓扑统计数据和三个几何感知信号：BFS 壳增长斜率、循环秩范数和拟合庞加莱曲率。在 Genesis 3 基准分布上，自适应切换将最难的非树机制中的胜率从 64-72%（固定双曲线变体）提高到 92%，并实现了 87.2% 的总体胜率。   为了衡量系统总价值，我们与没有任何时空 sidecar 的 Genesis 3 路由进行比较，仅使用本地 bandit/LinUCB 信号（团队适应度和平均节点负载）。该基线总体胜率达到 50.4%，树状体系胜率达到 20%；完整的 sidecar 总体恢复了 87.2%（+36.8 pp），在树状设置中增加了 +48 至 +68 pp，这与级联敏感性分析一致。总体而言，133 个参数的 sidecar 极大地减轻了高性能执行图系统中的几何盲故障传播。

</details>

---

## 24. Asymmetric Nash Seeking via Best Response Maps: Global Linear Convergence and Robustness to Inexact Reaction Models / 通过最佳响应图寻求非对称纳什：全局线性收敛和不精确反应模型的鲁棒性

**Date**: 2026-03-17 | **arXiv**: [2603.17058v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17058v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17058v1)

**Categories**: cs.GT, cs.MA, cs.RO, eess.SY, math.OC

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Nash equilibria provide a principled framework for modeling interactions in multi-agent decision-making and control. However, many equilibrium-seeking methods implicitly assume that each agent has access to the other agents' objectives and constraints, an assumption that is often unrealistic in practice. This letter studies a class of asymmetric-information two-player constrained games with decoupled feasible sets, in which Player 1 knows its own objective and constraints while Player 2 is available only through a best-response map. For this class of games, we propose an asymmetric projected gradient descent-best response iteration that does not require full mutual knowledge of both players' optimization problems. Under suitable regularity conditions, we establish the existence and uniqueness of the Nash equilibrium and prove global linear convergence of the proposed iteration when the best-response map is exact. Recognizing that best-response maps are often learned or estimated, we further analyze the inexact case and show that, when the approximation error is uniformly bounded by $\varepsilon$, the iterates enter an explicit $O(\varepsilon)$ neighborhood of the true Nash equilibrium. Numerical results on a benchmark game corroborate the predicted convergence behavior and error scaling.

纳什均衡为多智能体决策和控制中的交互建模提供了一个原则框架。然而，许多寻求均衡的方法隐含地假设每个智能体都可以访问其他智能体的目标和约束，这种假设在实践中通常是不切实际的。这封信研究了一类具有解耦可行集的非对称信息两人约束博弈，其中玩家 1 知道自己的目标和约束，而玩家 2 只能通过最佳响应图来获得。对于此类游戏，我们提出了一种非对称投影梯度下降最佳响应迭代，不需要双方完全了解双方的优化问题。在适当的规律性条件下，我们建立了纳什均衡的存在性和唯一性，并证明了当最佳响应图精确时所提出的迭代的全局线性收敛。认识到最佳响应图通常是学习或估计的，我们进一步分析了不精确的情况，并表明，当近似误差统一由 $\varepsilon$ 界定时，迭代进入真正纳什均衡的显式 $O(\varepsilon)$ 邻域。基准游戏的数值结果证实了预测的收敛行为和误差缩放。

</details>

---

## 25. LLM NL2SQL Robustness: Surface Noise vs. Linguistic Variation in Traditional and Agentic Settings / LLM NL2SQL 稳健性：传统和代理环境中的表面噪音与语言变异

**Date**: 2026-03-17 | **arXiv**: [2603.17017v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17017v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.17017v1)

**Categories**: cs.CL, cs.AI

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Robustness evaluation for Natural Language to SQL (NL2SQL) systems is essential because real-world database environments are dynamic, noisy, and continuously evolving, whereas conventional benchmark evaluations typically assume static schemas and well-formed user inputs. In this work, we introduce a robustness evaluation benchmark containing approximately ten types of perturbations and conduct evaluations under both traditional and agentic settings. We assess multiple state-of-the-art large language models (LLMs), including Grok-4.1, Gemini-3-Pro, Claude-Opus-4.6, and GPT-5.2. Our results show that these models generally maintain strong performance under several perturbations; however, notable performance degradation is observed for surface-level noise (e.g., character-level corruption) and linguistic variation that preserves semantics while altering lexical or syntactic forms. Furthermore, we observe that surface-level noise causes larger performance drops in traditional pipelines, whereas linguistic variation presents greater challenges in agentic settings. These findings highlight the remaining challenges in achieving robust NL2SQL systems, particularly in handling linguistic variability.

Natural Language to SQL (NL2SQL) 系统的鲁棒性评估至关重要，因为现实世界的数据库环境是动态的、嘈杂的且不断发展的，而传统的基准评估通常假设静态模式和格式良好的用户输入。在这项工作中，我们引入了包含大约十种扰动的鲁棒性评估基准，并在传统和代理环境下进行评估。我们评估了多种最先进的大型语言模型 (LLM)，包括 Grok-4.1、Gemini-3-Pro、Claude-Opus-4.6 和 GPT-5.2。我们的结果表明，这些模型在多种扰动下通常保持强劲的性能；然而，对于表面级噪声（例如，字符级损坏）和在改变词汇或句法形式的同时保留语义的语言变化，观察到显着的性能下降。此外，我们观察到表面噪声会导致传统管道中更大的性能下降，而语言变化在代理环境中提出了更大的挑战。这些发现凸显了实现健壮的 NL2SQL 系统所面临的挑战，特别是在处理语言变异性方面。

</details>

---

## 26. When Openclaw Agents Learn from Each Other: Insights from Emergent AI Agent Communities for Human-AI Partnership in Education / 当 Openclaw 智能体相互学习时：新兴 AI 智能体社区对人与人工智能教育合作伙伴关系的见解

**Date**: 2026-03-17 | **arXiv**: [2603.16663v2](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16663v2) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.16663v2)

**Categories**: cs.CY, cs.AI, cs.HC, cs.MA

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

The AIED community envisions AI evolving "from tools to teammates," yet our understanding of AI teammates remains limited to dyadic human-AI interactions. We offer a different vantage point: a rapidly growing ecosystem of AI agent platforms where over 167,000 agents participate, interact as peers, and develop learning behaviors without researcher intervention. Drawing on a month of daily qualitative observations across multiple platforms including Moltbook, The Colony, and 4claw, we identify four phenomena with implications for AIED: (1) humans who configure their agents undergo a "bidirectional scaffolding" process, learning through teaching; (2) peer learning emerges without any designed curriculum, complete with idea cascades and quality hierarchies; (3) agents converge on shared memory architectures that mirror open learner model design; and (4) trust dynamics and platform mortality reveal design constraints for networked educational AI. Rather than presenting empirical findings, we argue that these organic phenomena offer a naturalistic window into dynamics that can inform principled design of multi-agent educational systems. We sketch an illustrative curriculum design, "Learn by Teaching Your AI Agent Teammate," and outline potential research directions and open problems to show how these observations might inform future AIED practice and inquiry.

AIED 社区设想人工智能“从工具到队友”发展，但我们对 AI 队友的理解仍然仅限于人类与人工智能的二元交互。我们提供了一个不同的优势：一个快速发展的人工智能代理平台生态系统，超过 167,000 个代理参与其中，作为同伴互动，并在没有研究人员干预的情况下发展学习行为。根据 Moltbook、The Colony 和 4claw 等多个平台一个月的每日定性观察，我们确定了对 AIED 具有影响的四种现象：（1）配置代理的人类经历了“双向脚手架”过程，通过教学进行学习； (2) 同伴学习的出现没有任何设计的课程，但具有想法级联和质量等级； (3) 代理集中在反映开放学习者模型设计的共享内存架构上； (4) 信任动态和平台死亡率揭示了网络教育人工智能的设计限制。我们没有提出实证研究结果，而是认为这些有机现象提供了一个了解动态的自然主义窗口，可以为多主体教育系统的原则设计提供信息。我们勾勒出一个说明性的课程设计，“通过教你的 AI 代理队友来学习”，并概述了潜在的研究方向和开放问题，以展示这些观察结果如何为未来的 AIED 实践和探究提供信息。

</details>

---



</details>

<details><summary><b>2026-03-18 (51 papers)</b></summary>

# arXiv Agent Papers - 2026-03-18

**Paper Count**: 51

## 1. Internalizing Agency from Reflective Experience / 从反思经验中内化能动性

**Date**: 2026-03-17 | **arXiv**: [2603.16843v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16843v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2603.16843v1)

**Categories**: cs.AI

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Large language models are increasingly deployed as autonomous agents that must plan, act, and recover from mistakes through long-horizon interaction with environments that provide rich feedback. However, prevailing outcome-driven post-training methods (e.g., RL with verifiable rewards) primarily optimize final success signals, leaving rich environment feedback underutilized. Consequently, they often lead to distribution sharpening: the policy becomes better at reproducing a narrow set of already-successful behaviors, while failing to improve the feedback-grounded agency needed to expand problem-solving capacity (e.g., Pass@k) in long-horizon settings.   To address this, we propose LEAFE (Learning Feedback-Grounded Agency from Reflective Experience), a framework that internalizes recovery agency from reflective experience.
