**Date**: 2026-02-26 | **arXiv**: [2602.22755v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2602.22755v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.22755v1)

**Categories**: cs.CL

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

We introduce AuditBench, an alignment auditing benchmark. AuditBench consists of 56 language models with implanted hidden behaviors. Each model has one of 14 concerning behaviors--such as sycophantic deference, opposition to AI regulation, or secret geopolitical loyalties--which it does not confess to when directly asked. AuditBench models are highly diverse--some are subtle, while others are overt, and we use varying training techniques both for implanting behaviors and training models not to confess. To demonstrate AuditBench's utility, we develop an investigator agent that autonomously employs a configurable set of auditing tools. By measuring investigator agent success using different tools, we can evaluate their efficacy. Notably, we observe a tool-to-agent gap, where tools that perform well in standalone non-agentic evaluations fail to translate into improved performance when used with our investigator agent. We find that our most effective tools involve scaffolded calls to auxiliary models that generate diverse prompts for the target. White-box interpretability tools can be helpful, but the agent performs best with black-box tools. We also find that audit success varies greatly across training techniques: models trained on synthetic documents are easier to audit than models trained on demonstrations, with better adversarial training further increasing auditing difficulty. We release our models, agent, and evaluation framework to support future quantitative, iterative science on alignment auditing.

我们推出 AuditBench，一种一致性审核基准。 AuditBench 由 56 个植入隐藏行为的语言模型组成。每个模型都有 14 种相关行为中的一种——例如阿谀奉承、反对人工智能监管或秘密的地缘政治忠诚——当被直接询问时，它不会承认这些行为。 AuditBench 模型高度多样化——有些是微妙的，有些是公开的，我们使用不同的训练技术来植入行为和训练模型不坦白。为了演示 AuditBench 的实用性，我们开发了一个调查代理，它可以自主地使用一组可配置的审计工具。通过使用不同的工具衡量调查人员的成功，我们可以评估他们的功效。值得注意的是，我们观察到工具与代理之间的差距，在独立非代理评估中表现良好的工具在与我们的调查代理一起使用时无法转化为性能的提高。我们发现我们最有效的工具涉及对辅助模型的脚手架调用，这些模型为目标生成不同的提示。白盒可解释性工具可能会有所帮助，但代理在使用黑盒工具时表现最佳。我们还发现，不同培训技术的审计成功率差异很大：在合成文档上训练的模型比在演示上训练的模型更容易审计，更好的对抗性训练进一步增加了审计难度。我们发布了我们的模型、代理和评估框架，以支持未来关于一致性审计的定量、迭代科学。

</details>

---

## 18. Pixel2Catch: Multi-Agent Sim-to-Real Transfer for Agile Manipulation with a Single RGB Camera / Pixel2Catch：多智能体模拟到真实传输，使用单个 RGB 相机进行敏捷操作

**Date**: 2026-02-26 | **arXiv**: [2602.22733v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2602.22733v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.22733v1)

**Categories**: cs.RO

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

To catch a thrown object, a robot must be able to perceive the object's motion and generate control actions in a timely manner. Rather than explicitly estimating the object's 3D position, this work focuses on a novel approach that recognizes object motion using pixel-level visual information extracted from a single RGB image. Such visual cues capture changes in the object's position and scale, allowing the policy to reason about the object's motion. Furthermore, to achieve stable learning in a high-DoF system composed of a robot arm equipped with a multi-fingered hand, we design a heterogeneous multi-agent reinforcement learning framework that defines the arm and hand as independent agents with distinct roles. Each agent is trained cooperatively using role-specific observations and rewards, and the learned policies are successfully transferred from simulation to the real world.

为了接住抛出的物体，机器人必须能够感知物体的运动并及时产生控制动作。这项工作不是明确估计对象的 3D 位置，而是重点关注一种使用从单个 RGB 图像中提取的像素级视觉信息来识别对象运动的新颖方法。此类视觉线索捕获对象位置和比例的变化，使策略能够推断对象的运动。此外，为了在由配备多指手的机器人手臂组成的高自由度系统中实现稳定学习，我们设计了一种异构多智能体强化学习框架，将手臂和手定义为具有不同角色的独立智能体。每个代理都使用特定于角色的观察和奖励进行协作训练，并且学习到的策略成功地从模拟转移到现实世界。

</details>

---

## 19. Toward Personalized LLM-Powered Agents: Foundations, Evaluation, and Future Directions / 走向个性化的法学硕士代理人：基础、评估和未来方向

**Date**: 2026-02-26 | **arXiv**: [2602.22680v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2602.22680v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.22680v1)

**Categories**: cs.AI

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Large language models have enabled agents that reason, plan, and interact with tools and environments to accomplish complex tasks. As these agents operate over extended interaction horizons, their effectiveness increasingly depends on adapting behavior to individual users and maintaining continuity across time, giving rise to personalized LLM-powered agents. In such long-term, user-dependent settings, personalization permeates the entire decision pipeline rather than remaining confined to surface-level generation. This survey provides a capability-oriented review of personalized LLM-powered agents. We organize the literature around four interdependent components: profile modeling, memory, planning, and action execution. Using this taxonomy, we synthesize representative methods and analyze how user signals are represented, propagated, and utilized, highlighting cross-component interactions and recurring design trade-offs. We further examine evaluation metrics and benchmarks tailored to personalized agents, summarize application scenarios spanning general assistance to specialized domains, and outline future directions for research and deployment. By offering a structured framework for understanding and designing personalized LLM-powered agents, this survey charts a roadmap toward more user-aligned, adaptive, robust, and deployable agentic systems, accelerating progress from prototype personalization to scalable real-world assistants.

大型语言模型使智能体能够推理、规划并与工具和环境交互，以完成复杂的任务。随着这些代理在扩展的交互范围内运行，它们的有效性越来越依赖于适应个体用户的行为并保持跨时间的连续性，从而产生了由法学硕士支持的个性化代理。在这种长期的、依赖于用户的设置中，个性化渗透到整个决策流程，而不是仅限于表面级别的生成。这项调查对拥有法学硕士学位的个性化代理人进行了以能力为导向的审查。我们围绕四个相互依赖的组件组织文献：概要建模、记忆、规划和行动执行。使用这种分类法，我们综合了代表性方法并分析了用户信号的表示、传播和利用方式，突出了跨组件交互和重复设计权衡。我们进一步研究针对个性化代理量身定制的评估指标和基准，总结从一般协助到专业领域的应用场景，并概述未来的研究和部署方向。通过提供一个结构化框架来理解和设计由法学硕士支持的个性化代理，这项调查绘制了一个路线图，旨在实现更加用户一致、自适应、强大和可部署的代理系统，加速从原型个性化到可扩展的现实世界助手的进展。

</details>

---

## 20. Search More, Think Less: Rethinking Long-Horizon Agentic Search for Efficiency and Generalization / 多搜索，少思考：重新思考长期代理搜索的效率和泛化

**Date**: 2026-02-26 | **arXiv**: [2602.22675v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2602.22675v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.22675v1)

**Categories**: cs.CL

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Recent deep research agents primarily improve performance by scaling reasoning depth, but this leads to high inference cost and latency in search-intensive scenarios. Moreover, generalization across heterogeneous research settings remains challenging. In this work, we propose \emph{Search More, Think Less} (SMTL), a framework for long-horizon agentic search that targets both efficiency and generalization. SMTL replaces sequential reasoning with parallel evidence acquisition, enabling efficient context management under constrained context budgets. To support generalization across task types, we further introduce a unified data synthesis pipeline that constructs search tasks spanning both deterministic question answering and open-ended research scenarios with task appropriate evaluation metrics. We train an end-to-end agent using supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning, achieving strong and often state of the art performance across benchmarks including BrowseComp (48.6\%), GAIA (75.7\%), Xbench (82.0\%), and DeepResearch Bench (45.9\%). Compared to Mirothinker-v1.0, SMTL with maximum 100 interaction steps reduces the average number of reasoning steps on BrowseComp by 70.7\%, while improving accuracy.

最近的深度研究代理主要通过扩展推理深度来提高性能，但这会导致搜索密集型场景中的推理成本和延迟较高。此外，跨异构研究环境的泛化仍然具有挑战性。在这项工作中，我们提出了\emph{Search More，Think Less}（SMTL），这是一个以效率和泛化为目标的长视野代理搜索框架。 SMTL 用并行证据获取取代顺序推理，从而在上下文预算有限的情况下实现高效的上下文管理。为了支持跨任务类型的泛化，我们进一步引入了一个统一的数据合成管道，该管道构建了跨越确定性问答和开放式研究场景的搜索任务，并具有适当的任务评估指标。我们使用监督微调和强化学习来训练端到端代理，在 BrowseComp (48.6\%)、GAIA (75.7\%)、Xbench (82.0\%) 和 DeepResearch Bench (45.9\%) 等基准测试中实现强大且最先进的性能。与 Mirothinker-v1.0 相比，最多 100 个交互步骤的 SMTL 将 BrowseComp 上的平均推理步骤数减少了 70.7%，同时提高了准确性。

</details>

---

## 21. MobilityBench: A Benchmark for Evaluating Route-Planning Agents in Real-World Mobility Scenarios / MobilityBench：评估真实移动场景中的路线规划代理的基准

**Date**: 2026-02-26 | **arXiv**: [2602.22638v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2602.22638v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.22638v1)

**Categories**: cs.AI

**Code**: https://github.com/AMAP-ML/MobilityBench

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Route-planning agents powered by large language models (LLMs) have emerged as a promising paradigm for supporting everyday human mobility through natural language interaction and tool-mediated decision making. However, systematic evaluation in real-world mobility settings is hindered by diverse routing demands, non-deterministic mapping services, and limited reproducibility. In this study, we introduce MobilityBench, a scalable benchmark for evaluating LLM-based route-planning agents in real-world mobility scenarios. MobilityBench is constructed from large-scale, anonymized real user queries collected from Amap and covers a broad spectrum of route-planning intents across multiple cities worldwide. To enable reproducible, end-to-end evaluation, we design a deterministic API-replay sandbox that eliminates environmental variance from live services. We further propose a multi-dimensional evaluation protocol centered on outcome validity, complemented by assessments of instruction understanding, planning, tool use, and efficiency. Using MobilityBench, we evaluate multiple LLM-based route-planning agents across diverse real-world mobility scenarios and provide an in-depth analysis of their behaviors and performance. Our findings reveal that current models perform competently on Basic information retrieval and Route Planning tasks, yet struggle considerably with Preference-Constrained Route Planning, underscoring significant room for improvement in personalized mobility applications. We publicly release the benchmark data, evaluation toolkit, and documentation at https://github.com/AMAP-ML/MobilityBench .

由大语言模型 (LLM) 支持的路线规划代理已成为通过自然语言交互和工具介导的决策来支持日常人类移动的有前景的范例。然而，现实世界移动环境中的系统评估受到不同路线需求、不确定性地图服务和有限再现性的阻碍。在本研究中，我们介绍了 MobilityBench，这是一个可扩展的基准，用于在现实移动场景中评估基于 LLM 的路线规划代理。 MobilityBench是根据从高德地图收集的大规模匿名真实用户查询构建的，涵盖了全球多个城市的广泛路线规划意图。为了实现可重复的端到端评估，我们设计了一个确定性的 API 重放沙箱，消除了实时服务的环境差异。我们进一步提出了一个以结果有效性为中心的多维评估方案，辅以对教学理解、规划、工具使用和效率的评估。使用 MobilityBench，我们在不同的现实世界移动场景中评估多个基于 LLM 的路线规划代理，并对其行为和性能进行深入分析。我们的研究结果表明，当前模型在基本信息检索和路线规划任务上表现良好，但在偏好受限的路线规划方面表现不佳，这凸显了个性化移动应用程序的巨大改进空间。我们在 https://github.com/AMAP-ML/MobilityBench 公开发布基准数据、评估工具包和文档。

</details>

---

## 22. SideQuest: Model-Driven KV Cache Management for Long-Horizon Agentic Reasoning / SideQuest：用于长视野代理推理的模型驱动的 KV 缓存管理

**Date**: 2026-02-26 | **arXiv**: [2602.22603v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2602.22603v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.22603v1)

**Categories**: cs.AI, cs.LG

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Long-running agentic tasks, such as deep research, require multi-hop reasoning over information distributed across multiple webpages and documents. In such tasks, the LLM context is dominated by tokens from external retrieval, causing memory usage to grow rapidly and limiting decode performance. While several KV cache compression techniques exist for long-context inputs, we find that existing heuristics fail to support multi-step reasoning models effectively. We address this challenge with SideQuest -- a novel approach that leverages the Large Reasoning Model (LRM) itself to perform KV cache compression by reasoning about the usefulness of tokens in its context. To prevent the tokens associated with this management process from polluting the model's memory, we frame KV cache compression as an auxiliary task executed in parallel to the main reasoning task. Our evaluations, using a model trained with just 215 samples, show that SideQuest reduces peak token usage by up to 65% on agentic tasks with minimal degradation in accuracy, outperforming heuristic-based KV cache compression techniques.

长时间运行的代理任务（例如深度研究）需要对分布在多个网页和文档中的信息进行多跳推理。在此类任务中，LLM 上下文主要由外部检索的标记主导，导致内存使用量快速增长并限制解码性能。虽然存在多种针对长上下文输入的 KV 缓存压缩技术，但我们发现现有的启发式方法无法有效支持多步骤推理模型。我们通过 SideQuest 解决了这一挑战，这是一种新颖的方法，它利用大型推理模型 (LRM) 本身，通过推理令牌在其上下文中的有用性来执行 KV 缓存压缩。为了防止与此管理过程相关的令牌污染模型的内存，我们将 KV 缓存压缩设计为与主要推理任务并行执行的辅助任务。我们使用仅用 215 个样本训练的模型进行的评估表明，SideQuest 将代理任务的峰值令牌使用量降低了 65%，同时准确率下降最小，优于基于启发式的 KV 缓存压缩技术。

</details>

---

## 23. Search-P1: Path-Centric Reward Shaping for Stable and Efficient Agentic RAG Training / Search-P1：以路径为中心的奖励塑造，实现稳定高效的代理 RAG 训练

**Date**: 2026-02-26 | **arXiv**: [2602.22576v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2602.22576v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.22576v1)

**Categories**: cs.CL, cs.IR, cs.LG

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) enhances large language models (LLMs) by incorporating external knowledge, yet traditional single-round retrieval struggles with complex multi-step reasoning. Agentic RAG addresses this by enabling LLMs to dynamically decide when and what to retrieve, but current RL-based training methods suffer from sparse outcome rewards that discard intermediate signals and low sample efficiency where failed samples contribute nothing. We propose Search-P1, a framework that introduces path-centric reward shaping for agentic RAG training, comprising two key components: (1) Path-Centric Reward, which evaluates the structural quality of reasoning trajectories through order-agnostic step coverage and soft scoring that extracts learning signals even from failed samples, and (2) Dual-Track Path Scoring with offline-generated reference planners that assesses paths from both self-consistency and reference-alignment perspectives. Experiments on multiple QA benchmarks demonstrate that Search-P1 achieves significant improvements over Search-R1 and other strong baselines, with an average accuracy gain of 7.7 points.

检索增强生成（RAG）通过整合外部知识来增强大型语言模型（LLM），但传统的单轮检索难以应对复杂的多步骤推理。 Agentic RAG 通过使 LLM 能够动态决定何时检索以及检索什么内容来解决这个问题，但当前基于 RL 的训练方法受到稀疏结果奖励的困扰，这些奖励会丢弃中间信号，并且样本效率低，其中失败的样本没有任何贡献。我们提出了 Search-P1，一个为代理 RAG 训练引入以路径为中心的奖励塑造的框架，包括两个关键组件：（1）以路径为中心的奖励，通过与顺序无关的步骤覆盖和软评分来评估推理轨迹的结构质量，甚至可以从失败的样本中提取学习信号；（2）使用离线生成的参考规划器进行双轨路径评分，从自一致性和参考对齐的角度评估路径。在多个 QA 基准上的实验表明，Search-P1 比 Search-R1 和其他强基线取得了显着改进，平均准确度提高了 7.7 点。

</details>

---

## 24. CourtGuard: A Model-Agnostic Framework for Zero-Shot Policy Adaptation in LLM Safety / CourtGuard：LLM 安全中零样本策略适应的模型不可知框架

**Date**: 2026-02-26 | **arXiv**: [2602.22557v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2602.22557v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.22557v1)

**Categories**: cs.AI, cs.LG

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Current safety mechanisms for Large Language Models (LLMs) rely heavily on static, fine-tuned classifiers that suffer from adaptation rigidity, the inability to enforce new governance rules without expensive retraining. To address this, we introduce CourtGuard, a retrieval-augmented multi-agent framework that reimagines safety evaluation as Evidentiary Debate. By orchestrating an adversarial debate grounded in external policy documents, CourtGuard achieves state-of-the-art performance across 7 safety benchmarks, outperforming dedicated policy-following baselines without fine-tuning. Beyond standard metrics, we highlight two critical capabilities: (1) Zero-Shot Adaptability, where our framework successfully generalized to an out-of-domain Wikipedia Vandalism task (achieving 90\% accuracy) by swapping the reference policy; and (2) Automated Data Curation and Auditing, where we leveraged CourtGuard to curate and audit nine novel datasets of sophisticated adversarial attacks. Our results demonstrate that decoupling safety logic from model weights offers a robust, interpretable, and adaptable path for meeting current and future regulatory requirements in AI governance.

当前大型语言模型 (LLM) 的安全机制严重依赖于静态的、经过微调的分类器，这些分类器存在适应僵化的问题，无法在不进行昂贵的再培训的情况下执行新的治理规则。为了解决这个问题，我们引入了 CourtGuard，这是一种检索增强的多智能体框架，它将安全评估重新想象为证据辩论。通过精心策划基于外部政策文件的对抗性辩论，CourtGuard 在 7 个安全基准上实现了最先进的性能，在无需微调的情况下超越了专门的政策遵循基准。除了标准指标之外，我们还强调了两个关键功能：（1）零样本适应性，我们的框架通过交换参考策略成功地推广到域外维基百科破坏任务（达到 90% 的准确率）； (2) 自动化数据管理和审计，我们利用 CourtGuard 来管理和审计复杂对抗性攻击的九个新颖数据集。我们的结果表明，将安全逻辑与模型权重解耦提供了一条稳健、可解释且适应性强的路径，可以满足人工智能治理中当前和未来的监管要求。

</details>

---

## 25. Agentic AI for Intent-driven Optimization in Cell-free O-RAN / 用于无细胞 O-RAN 中意图驱动优化的代理 AI

**Date**: 2026-02-26 | **arXiv**: [2602.22539v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2602.22539v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.22539v1)

**Categories**: cs.AI, eess.SP

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Agentic artificial intelligence (AI) is emerging as a key enabler for autonomous radio access networks (RANs), where multiple large language model (LLM)-based agents reason and collaborate to achieve operator-defined intents. The open RAN (O-RAN) architecture enables the deployment and coordination of such agents. However, most existing works consider simple intents handled by independent agents, while complex intents that require coordination among agents remain unexplored. In this paper, we propose an agentic AI framework for intent translation and optimization in cell-free O-RAN. A supervisor agent translates the operator intents into an optimization objective and minimum rate requirements. Based on this information, a user weighting agent retrieves relevant prior experience from a memory module to determine the user priority weights for precoding. If the intent includes an energy-saving objective, then an open radio unit (O-RU) management agent will also be activated to determine the set of active O-RUs by using a deep reinforcement learning (DRL) algorithm. A monitoring agent measures and monitors the user data rates and coordinates with other agents to guarantee the minimum rate requirements are satisfied. To enhance scalability, we adopt a parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) method that enables the same underlying LLM to be used for different agents. Simulation results show that the proposed agentic AI framework reduces the number of active O-RUs by 41.93% when compared with three baseline schemes in energy-saving mode. Using the PEFT method, the proposed framework reduces the memory usage by 92% when compared with deploying separate LLM agents.

代理人工智能 (AI) 正在成为自主无线电接入网络 (RAN) 的关键推动者，其中多个基于大型语言模型 (LLM) 的代理进行推理和协作，以实现运营商定义的意图。开放式 RAN (O-RAN) 架构支持此类代理的部署和协调。然而，大多数现有工作考虑由独立代理处理的简单意图，而需要代理之间协调的复杂意图仍未被探索。在本文中，我们提出了一种用于无细胞 O-RAN 中意图翻译和优化的代理 AI 框架。主管代理将操作员意图转化为优化目标和最低速率要求。基于该信息，用户加权代理从存储器模块检索相关先前经验以确定用于预编码的用户优先级权重。如果意图包括节能目标，则还将激活开放无线电单元（O-RU）管理代理，以通过使用深度强化学习（DRL）算法来确定活动O-RU集合。监控代理测量和监控用户数据速率并与其他代理协调以保证满足最低速率要求。为了增强可扩展性，我们采用参数高效微调（PEFT）方法，使相同的底层 LLM 能够用于不同的代理。仿真结果表明，与节能模式下的三种基线方案相比，所提出的代理AI框架将活动O-RU的数量减少了41.93%。使用 PEFT 方法，与部署单独的 LLM 代理相比，所提出的框架减少了 92% 的内存使用量。

</details>

---

## 26. Silent Egress: When Implicit Prompt Injection Makes LLM Agents Leak Without a Trace / 无声出口：当隐式提示注入使 LLM 代理泄漏无痕时

**Date**: 2026-02-25 | **arXiv**: [2602.22450v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2602.22450v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.22450v1)

**Categories**: cs.CR, cs.AI

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Agentic large language model systems increasingly automate tasks by retrieving URLs and calling external tools. We show that this workflow gives rise to implicit prompt injection: adversarial instructions embedded in automatically generated URL previews, including titles, metadata, and snippets, can introduce a system-level risk that we refer to as silent egress. Using a fully local and reproducible testbed, we demonstrate that a malicious web page can induce an agent to issue outbound requests that exfiltrate sensitive runtime context, even when the final response shown to the user appears harmless. In 480 experimental runs with a qwen2.5:7b-based agent, the attack succeeds with high probability (P (egress) =0.89), and 95% of successful attacks are not detected by output-based safety checks. We also introduce sharded exfiltration, where sensitive information is split across multiple requests to avoid detection. This strategy reduces single-request leakage metrics by 73% (Leak@1) and bypasses simple data loss prevention mechanisms. Our ablation results indicate that defenses applied at the prompt layer offer limited protection, while controls at the system and network layers, such as domain allowlisting and redirect-chain analysis, are considerably more effective. These findings suggest that network egress should be treated as a first-class security outcome in agentic LLM systems. We outline architectural directions, including provenance tracking and capability isolation, that go beyond prompt-level hardening.

代理大型语言模型系统通过检索 URL 和调用外部工具来日益自动化任务。我们表明，此工作流程会产生隐式提示注入：自动生成的 URL 预览中嵌入的对抗性指令（包括标题、元数据和片段）可能会引入系统级风险，我们将其称为静默出口。使用完全本地且可重现的测试台，我们证明恶意网页可以诱导代理发出出站请求，从而泄露敏感的运行时上下文，即使向用户显示的最终响应看似无害。在使用基于 qwen2.5:7b 的代理进行的 480 次实验运行中，攻击成功的概率很高（P（出口）=0.89），并且 95% 的成功攻击未被基于输出的安全检查检测到。我们还引入了分片渗透，其中敏感信息被分割到多个请求中以避免检测。该策略将单请求泄漏指标减少了 73% (Leak@1)，并绕过了简单的数据丢失预防机制。我们的消融结果表明，在提示层应用的防御提供的保护有限，而系统和网络层的控制（例如域允许列表和重定向链分析）则要有效得多。这些发现表明，网络出口应被视为代理 LLM 系统中的一流安全结果。我们概述了超越即时级别强化的架构方向，包括来源跟踪和功能隔离。

</details>

---

## 27. A Framework for Assessing AI Agent Decisions and Outcomes in AutoML Pipelines / 用于评估 AutoML 管道中 AI 代理决策和结果的框架

**Date**: 2026-02-25 | **arXiv**: [2602.22442v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2602.22442v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.22442v1)

**Categories**: cs.AI

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Agent-based AutoML systems rely on large language models to make complex, multi-stage decisions across data processing, model selection, and evaluation. However, existing evaluation practices remain outcome-centric, focusing primarily on final task performance. Through a review of prior work, we find that none of the surveyed agentic AutoML systems report structured, decision-level evaluation metrics intended for post-hoc assessment of intermediate decision quality. To address this limitation, we propose an Evaluation Agent (EA) that performs decision-centric assessment of AutoML agents without interfering with their execution. The EA is designed as an observer that evaluates intermediate decisions along four dimensions: decision validity, reasoning consistency, model quality risks beyond accuracy, and counterfactual decision impact. Across four proof-of-concept experiments, we demonstrate that the EA can (i) detect faulty decisions with an F1 score of 0.919, (ii) identify reasoning inconsistencies independent of final outcomes, and (iii) attribute downstream performance changes to agent decisions, revealing impacts ranging from -4.9\% to +8.3\% in final metrics. These results illustrate how decision-centric evaluation exposes failure modes that are invisible to outcome-only metrics. Our work reframes the evaluation of agentic AutoML systems from an outcome-based perspective to one that audits agent decisions, offering a foundation for reliable, interpretable, and governable autonomous ML systems.

基于代理的 AutoML 系统依靠大型语言模型在数据处理、模型选择和评估方面做出复杂的多阶段决策。然而，现有的评估实践仍然以结果为中心，主要关注最终任务绩效。通过回顾之前的工作，我们发现所调查的代理 AutoML 系统都没有报告用于中间决策质量事后评估的结构化决策级评估指标。为了解决这一限制，我们提出了一种评估代理 (EA)，它可以对 AutoML 代理执行以决策为中心的评估，而不会干扰其执行。 EA 被设计为一个观察者，从四个维度评估中间决策：决策有效性、推理一致性、超出准确性的模型质量风险以及反事实决策影响。通过四个概念验证实验，我们证明 EA 可以 (i) 检测 F1 分数为 0.919 的错误决策，(ii) 识别与最终结果无关的推理不一致，以及 (iii) 将下游性能变化归因于代理决策，揭示最终指标中从 -4.9\% 到 +8.3\% 的影响。这些结果说明了以决策为中心的评估如何揭示仅结果指标不可见的故障模式。我们的工作将代理 AutoML 系统的评估从基于结果的角度重新构建为审核代理决策的角度，为可靠、可解释和可治理的自主 ML 系统奠定了基础。

</details>

---

## 28. ArchAgent: Agentic AI-driven Computer Architecture Discovery / ArchAgent：代理人工智能驱动的计算机架构发现

**Date**: 2026-02-25 | **arXiv**: [2602.22425v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2602.22425v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.22425v1)

**Categories**: cs.AI, cs.AR

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Agile hardware design flows are a critically needed force multiplier to meet the exploding demand for compute. Recently, agentic generative AI systems have demonstrated significant advances in algorithm design, improving code efficiency, and enabling discovery across scientific domains.   Bridging these worlds, we present ArchAgent, an automated computer architecture discovery system built on AlphaEvolve. We show ArchAgent's ability to automatically design/implement state-of-the-art (SoTA) cache replacement policies (architecting new mechanisms/logic, not only changing parameters), broadly within the confines of an established cache replacement policy design competition.   In two days without human intervention, ArchAgent generated a policy achieving a 5.3% IPC speedup improvement over the prior SoTA on public multi-core Google Workload Traces. On the heavily-explored single-core SPEC06 workloads, it generated a policy in just 18 days showing a 0.9% IPC speedup improvement over the existing SoTA (a similar "winning margin" as reported by the existing SoTA). ArchAgent achieved these gains 3-5x faster than prior human-developed SoTA policies.   Agentic flows also enable "post-silicon hyperspecialization" where agents tune runtime-configurable parameters exposed in hardware policies to further align the policies with a specific workload (mix). Exploiting this, we demonstrate a 2.4% IPC speedup improvement over prior SoTA on SPEC06 workloads.   Finally, we outline broader implications for computer architecture research in the era of agentic AI. For example, we demonstrate the phenomenon of "simulator escapes", where the agentic AI flow discovered and exploited a loophole in a popular microarchitectural simulator - a consequence of the fact that these research tools were designed for a (now past) world where they were exclusively operated by humans acting in good-faith.

敏捷硬件设计流程是满足爆炸性计算需求所迫切需要的力量倍增器。最近，代理生成式人工智能系统在算法设计、提高代码效率以及实现跨科学领域的发现方面取得了重大进展。   为了连接这些世界，我们推出了 ArchAgent，这是一个基于 AlphaEvolve 构建的自动化计算机架构发现系统。我们展示了 ArchAgent 自动设计/实现最先进 (SoTA) 缓存替换策略（构建新机制/逻辑，而不仅仅是更改参数）的能力，广泛地在已建立的缓存替换策略设计竞赛的范围内。   在没有人工干预的两天内，ArchAgent 生成了一项策略，在公共多核 Google Workload Traces 上实现了比之前的 SoTA 5.3% 的 IPC 加速改进。在经过深入探索的单核 SPEC06 工作负载上，它在短短 18 天内生成了一项策略，显示出比现有 SoTA 提高了 0.9% 的 IPC 加速（与现有 SoTA 报告的类似“获胜幅度”）。 ArchAgent 实现这些收益的速度比之前人类开发的 SoTA 策略快 3-5 倍。   代理流还支持“硅后超专业化”，其中代理调整硬件策略中公开的运行时可配置参数，以进一步使策略与特定工作负载（混合）保持一致。利用这一点，我们在 SPEC06 工作负载上证明了 IPC 加速比之前的 SoTA 提高了 2.4%。   最后，我们概述了代理人工智能时代对计算机体系结构研究的更广泛影响。例如，我们演示了“模拟器逃逸”现象，即代理人工智能流程发现并利用了流行的微架构模拟器中的漏洞，这是因为这些研究工具是为（现在已经过去）的世界而设计的，在这个世界中，它们完全由善意的人类操作。

</details>

---

## 29. Agent Behavioral Contracts: Formal Specification and Runtime Enforcement for Reliable Autonomous AI Agents / 代理行为契约：可靠的自主人工智能代理的正式规范和运行时执行

**Date**: 2026-02-25 | **arXiv**: [2602.22302v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2602.22302v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.22302v1)

**Categories**: cs.AI, cs.MA, cs.SE

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Traditional software relies on contracts -- APIs, type systems, assertions -- to specify and enforce correct behavior. AI agents, by contrast, operate on prompts and natural language instructions with no formal behavioral specification. This gap is the root cause of drift, governance failures, and frequent project failures in agentic AI deployments. We introduce Agent Behavioral Contracts (ABC), a formal framework that brings Design-by-Contract principles to autonomous AI agents. An ABC contract C = (P, I, G, R) specifies Preconditions, Invariants, Governance policies, and Recovery mechanisms as first-class, runtime-enforceable components. We define (p, delta, k)-satisfaction -- a probabilistic notion of contract compliance that accounts for LLM non-determinism and recovery -- and prove a Drift Bounds Theorem showing that contracts with recovery rate gamma > alpha (the natural drift rate) bound behavioral drift to D* = alpha/gamma in expectation, with Gaussian concentration in the stochastic setting. We establish sufficient conditions for safe contract composition in multi-agent chains and derive probabilistic degradation bounds. We implement ABC in AgentAssert, a runtime enforcement library, and evaluate on AgentContract-Bench, a benchmark of 200 scenarios across 7 models from 6 vendors. Results across 1,980 sessions show that contracted agents detect 5.2-6.8 soft violations per session that uncontracted baselines miss entirely (p < 0.0001, Cohen's d = 6.7-33.8), achieve 88-100% hard constraint compliance, and bound behavioral drift to D* < 0.27 across extended sessions, with 100% recovery for frontier models and 17-100% across all models, at overhead < 10 ms per action.

传统软件依赖契约——API、类型系统、断言——来指定和强制执行正确的行为。相比之下，人工智能代理根据提示和自然语言指令进行操作，没有正式的行为规范。这种差距是代理人工智能部署中出现偏差、治理失败和频繁项目失败的根本原因。我们引入了代理行为契约（ABC），这是一个正式的框架，它将契约设计原则引入了自主人工智能代理中。 ABC 合约 C = (P, I, G, R) 将先决条件、不变量、治理策略和恢复机制指定为一流的运行时可执行组件。我们定义 (p, delta, k)-满意度——合同合规性的概率概念，解释了 LLM 的非确定性和恢复——并证明了漂移界限定理，表明恢复率 gamma > alpha（自然漂移率）的合同将行为漂移绑定到预期的 D* = alpha/gamma，在随机设置中具有高斯集中度。我们为多智能体链中的安全合约构成建立了充分的条件，并推导出概率退化界限。我们在运行时执行库 AgentAssert 中实现 ABC，并在 AgentContract-Bench 上进行评估，这是来自 6 个供应商的 7 个模型的 200 个场景的基准。 1,980 个会话的结果显示，签约代理每次会话检测到 5.2-6.8 次软违规，而未签约基线完全错过（p < 0.0001，Cohen's d = 6.7-33.8），实现 88-100% 硬约束合规性，并将行为漂移限制在扩展会话中的 D* < 0.27，前沿模型恢复 100%，所有模型恢复 17-100%模型，每次操作开销 < 10 毫秒。

</details>

---

## 30. Hierarchical LLM-Based Multi-Agent Framework with Prompt Optimization for Multi-Robot Task Planning / 基于LLM的分层多智能体框架，可快速优化多机器人任务规划

**Date**: 2026-02-25 | **arXiv**: [2602.21670v2](http://arxiv.org/abs/2602.21670v2) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.21670v2)

**Categories**: cs.RO, cs.AI, cs.MA

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Multi-robot task planning requires decomposing natural-language instructions into executable actions for heterogeneous robot teams. Conventional Planning Domain Definition Language (PDDL) planners provide rigorous guarantees but struggle to handle ambiguous or long-horizon missions, while large language models (LLMs) can interpret instructions and propose plans but may hallucinate or produce infeasible actions. We present a hierarchical multi-agent LLM-based planner with prompt optimization: an upper layer decomposes tasks and assigns them to lower-layer agents, which generate PDDL problems solved by a classical planner. When plans fail, the system applies TextGrad-inspired textual-gradient updates to optimize each agent's prompt and thereby improve planning accuracy. In addition, meta-prompts are learned and shared across agents within the same layer, enabling efficient prompt optimization in multi-agent settings. On the MAT-THOR benchmark, our planner achieves success rates of 0.95 on compound tasks, 0.84 on complex tasks, and 0.60 on vague tasks, improving over the previous state-of-the-art LaMMA-P by 2, 7, and 15 percentage points respectively. An ablation study shows that the hierarchical structure, prompt optimization, and meta-prompt sharing contribute roughly +59, +37, and +4 percentage points to the overall success rate.

多机器人任务规划需要将自然语言指令分解为异构机器人团队的可执行动作。传统的规划领域定义语言（PDDL）规划者提供严格的保证，但难以处理模糊或长期的任务，而大型语言模型（LLM）可以解释指令并提出计划，但可能会产生幻觉或产生不可行的行动。我们提出了一种基于 LLM 的分层多智能体规划器，具有即时优化功能：上层分解任务并将其分配给下层智能体，从而生成由经典规划器解决的 PDDL 问题。当计划失败时，系统会应用受 TextGrad 启发的文本梯度更新来优化每个代理的提示，从而提高计划准确性。此外，元提示可以在同一层内的代理之间学习和共享，从而在多代理设置中实现有效的提示优化。在 MAT-THOR 基准上，我们的规划器在复合任务上实现了 0.95 的成功率，在复杂任务上实现了 0.84 的成功率，在模糊任务上实现了 0.60 的成功率，分别比之前最先进的 LaMMA-P 提高了 2、7 和 15 个百分点。消融研究表明，层次结构、提示优化和元提示共享对整体成功率的贡献大约为 +59、+37 和 +4 个百分点。

</details>

---

## 31. ParkDiffusion++: Ego Intention Conditioned Joint Multi-Agent Trajectory Prediction for Automated Parking using Diffusion Models / ParkDiffusion++：使用扩散模型进行自动停车的自我意图条件联合多智能体轨迹预测

**Date**: 2026-02-24 | **arXiv**: [2602.20923v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2602.20923v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.20923v1)

**Categories**: cs.RO

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Automated parking is a challenging operational domain for advanced driver assistance systems, requiring robust scene understanding and interaction reasoning. The key challenge is twofold: (i) predict multiple plausible ego intentions according to context and (ii) for each intention, predict the joint responses of surrounding agents, enabling effective what-if decision-making. However, existing methods often fall short, typically treating these interdependent problems in isolation. We propose ParkDiffusion++, which jointly learns a multi-modal ego intention predictor and an ego-conditioned multi-agent joint trajectory predictor for automated parking. Our approach makes several key contributions. First, we introduce an ego intention tokenizer that predicts a small set of discrete endpoint intentions from agent histories and vectorized map polylines. Second, we perform ego-intention-conditioned joint prediction, yielding socially consistent predictions of the surrounding agents for each possible ego intention. Third, we employ a lightweight safety-guided denoiser with different constraints to refine joint scenes during training, thus improving accuracy and safety. Fourth, we propose counterfactual knowledge distillation, where an EMA teacher refined by a frozen safety-guided denoiser provides pseudo-targets that capture how agents react to alternative ego intentions. Extensive evaluations demonstrate that ParkDiffusion++ achieves state-of-the-art performance on the Dragon Lake Parking (DLP) dataset and the Intersections Drone (inD) dataset. Importantly, qualitative what-if visualizations show that other agents react appropriately to different ego intentions.

对于高级驾驶辅助系统来说，自动停车是一个具有挑战性的操作领域，需要强大的场景理解和交互推理。关键挑战是双重的：(i) 根据上下文预测多个看似合理的自我意图，(ii) 对于每个意图，预测周围代理的联合反应，从而实现有效的假设决策。然而，现有的方法常常存在不足，通常是孤立地处理这些相互依赖的问题。我们提出 ParkDiffusion++，它联合学习用于自动停车的多模式自我意图预测器和自我条件多智能体联合轨迹预测器。我们的方法做出了几个关键贡献。首先，我们引入一个自我意图标记器，它根据代理历史和矢量化地图折线预测一小组离散端点意图。其次，我们执行以自我意图为条件的联合预测，针对每个可能的自我意图，对周围的代理产生社会一致的预测。第三，我们采用具有不同约束的轻量级安全引导降噪器来细化训练期间的联合场景，从而提高准确性和安全性。第四，我们提出反事实知识蒸馏，其中由冷冻安全引导降噪器提炼的 EMA 教师提供伪目标，捕获代理对替代自我意图的反应。广泛的评估表明 ParkDiffusion++ 在 Dragon Lake Parking (DLP) 数据集和 Intersections Drone (inD) 数据集上实现了最先进的性能。重要的是，定性假设可视化表明其他主体对不同的自我意图做出适当的反应。

</details>

---



</details>

<details><summary><b>2026-02-26 (3 papers)</b></summary>

# arXiv Agent Papers - 2026-02-26

**Paper Count**: 3

## 1. SWE-Protégé: Learning to Selectively Collaborate With an Expert Unlocks Small Language Models as Software Engineering Agents / SWE-Protégé：学习有选择地与专家合作，解锁作为软件工程代理的小语言模型

**Date**: 2026-02-25 | **arXiv**: [2602.22124v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2602.22124v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.22124v1)

**Categories**: cs.SE, cs.AI, cs.CL, cs.LG

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

Small language models (SLMs) offer compelling advantages in cost, latency, and adaptability, but have so far lagged behind larger models on long-horizon software engineering tasks such as SWE-bench, where they suffer from pervasive action looping and low resolution rates. We introduce SWE-Protégé, a post-training framework that reframes software repair as an expert-protégé collaboration problem. In SWE-Protégé, an SLM remains the sole decision-maker while learning to selectively seek guidance from a strong expert model, recognize stalled states, and follow through on expert feedback. Our approach combines supervised fine-tuning on expert-augmented trajectories with agentic reinforcement learning that explicitly discourages degenerative looping and unproductive expert collaboration. We lightly post-train Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct to achieve 42.4% Pass@1 on SWE-bench Verified, a +25.4% improvement over the prior SLM state of the art, while using expert assistance sparsely (~4 calls per task and 11% of total tokens).

小语言模型 (SLM) 在成本、延迟和适应性方面提供了引人注目的优势，但迄今为止在长期软件工程任务（例如 SWE-bench）上落后于较大的模型，在这些任务中，它们普遍存在动作循环和低分辨率的问题。我们引入了 SWE-Protégé，这是一个训练后框架，它将软件修复重新定义为专家与受保护者协作问题。在 SWE-Protégé 中，SLM 仍然是唯一的决策者，同时学习有选择地寻求强大专家模型的指导、识别停滞状态并遵循专家反馈。我们的方法将专家增强轨迹的监督微调与代理强化学习相结合，明确阻止退化循环和低效的专家协作。我们对 Qwen2.5-Coder-7B-Instruct 进行了轻微的后期训练，在 SWE-bench Verified 上实现了 42.4% Pass@1，比之前的 SLM 技术水平提高了 25.4%，同时很少使用专家协助（每个任务约 4 次调用，占总代币的 11%）。

</details>

---

## 2. Some Simple Economics of AGI / AGI 的一些简单经济学

**Date**: 2026-02-24 | **arXiv**: [2602.20946v2](http://arxiv.org/abs/2602.20946v2) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.20946v2)

**Categories**: econ.GN, cs.AI, cs.CY, cs.LG, cs.SI

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

For millennia, human cognition was the primary engine of progress on Earth. As AI decouples cognition from biology, the marginal cost of measurable execution falls to zero, absorbing any labor capturable by metrics--including creative, analytical, and innovative work. The binding constraint on growth is no longer intelligence but human verification bandwidth: the capacity to validate, audit, and underwrite responsibility when execution is abundant. We model the AGI transition as the collision of two racing cost curves: an exponentially decaying Cost to Automate and a biologically bottlenecked Cost to Verify. This structural asymmetry widens a Measurability Gap between what agents can execute and what humans can afford to verify. It also drives a shift from skill-biased to measurability-biased technical change. Rents migrate to verification-grade ground truth, cryptographic provenance, and liability underwriting--the ability to insure outcomes rather than merely generate them. The current human-in-the-loop equilibrium is unstable: eroded from below as apprenticeship collapses (Missing Junior Loop) and from within as experts codify their obsolescence (Codifier's Curse). Unverified deployment becomes privately rational--a Trojan Horse externality. Unmanaged, these forces pull toward a Hollow Economy. Yet by scaling verification alongside agentic capabilities, the forces that threaten collapse become the catalyst for unbounded discovery and experimentation--an Augmented Economy. We derive a practical playbook for individuals, companies, investors, and policymakers. Today's defining challenge is not the race to deploy the most autonomous systems; it is the race to secure the foundations of their oversight. Only by scaling our bandwidth for verification alongside our capacity for execution can we ensure that the intelligence we have summoned preserves the humanity that initiated it.

几千年来，人类认知是地球进步的主要引擎。随着人工智能将认知与生物学脱钩，可测量执行的边际成本降至零，吸收了可通过指标捕获的任何劳动力，包括创造性、分析性和创新性工作。对增长的约束不再是智力，而是人类验证带宽：在执行充足时验证、审计和承担责任的能力。我们将 AGI 转变建模为两条竞赛成本曲线的碰撞：指数衰减的自动化成本和生物瓶颈验证成本。这种结构上的不对称扩大了代理可以执行的内容和人类可以验证的内容之间的可测量性差距。它还推动了从偏向技能到偏向可衡量的技术变革的转变。租金转向验证级的基本事实、加密来源和责任承保——确保结果而不仅仅是产生结果的能力。当前的人机循环平衡是不稳定的：随着学徒制的崩溃（初级循环缺失）而从底层受到侵蚀，随着专家将其过时编入法典（编纂者的诅咒）而从内部受到侵蚀。未经验证的部署变得私人理性——特洛伊木马外部性。如果不加以管理，这些力量就会走向空心经济。然而，通过扩大验证和代理能力，威胁崩溃的力量成为无限发现和实验的催化剂——增强经济。我们为个人、公司、投资者和政策制定者提供了一本实用的策略。当今的决定性挑战不是部署最自治系统的竞赛，而是部署最自治系统的竞赛。这是一场确保其监督基础的竞赛。只有通过扩展我们的验证带宽和我们的执行能力，我们才能确保我们所召唤的情报能够保护发起它的人性。

</details>

---

## 3. Structured Prompt Language: Declarative Context Management for LLMs / 结构化提示语言：法学硕士的声明性上下文管理

**Date**: 2026-02-23 | **arXiv**: [2602.21257v1](http://arxiv.org/abs/2602.21257v1) | **PDF**: [Link](http://arxiv.org/pdf/2602.21257v1)

**Categories**: cs.CL, cs.DB, cs.PL

<details><summary><b>Abstract / 摘要</b></summary>

We present SPL (Structured Prompt Language), a declarative SQL-inspired language that treats large language models as generative knowledge bases and their context windows as constrained resources. SPL provides explicit WITH BUDGET/LIMIT token management, an automatic query optimizer, EXPLAIN transparency analogous to SQL's EXPLAIN ANALYZE, and native integration of retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) and persistent memory in a single declarative framework. SPL-flow extends SPL into resilient agentic pipelines with a three-tier provider fallback strategy (Ollama -> OpenRouter -> self-healing retry) fully transparent to the .spl script. Five extensions demonstrate the paradigm's breadth: (1) Text2SPL (multilingual NL->SPL translation); (2) Mixture-of-Models (MoM) routing that dispatches each PROMPT to a domain-specialist model at runtime; (3) Logical Chunking, an intelligent strategy for documents exceeding a single context window--expressed naturally through SPL's existing CTE syntax with no new constructs, decomposing a large query into a Map-Reduce pipeline that reduces attention cost from O(N^2) to O(N^2/k) and runs identically on cloud (parallel) or local hardware (sequential); (4) SPL-flow, a declarative agentic orchestration layer with resilient three-tier provider fallback; and (5) BENCHMARK for parallel multi-model comparison with automatic winner persistence. We provide a formal EBNF grammar, two pip-installable Python packages (spl-llm, spl-flow), and comparison against Prompty, DSPy, and LMQL. SPL reduces prompt boilerplate by 65% on average, surfaces a 68x cost spread across model tiers as a pre-execution signal, and runs the identical .spl script at $0.002 on OpenRouter or at zero marginal cost on a local Ollama instance--without modification.

我们提出了 SPL（结构化提示语言），这是一种受 SQL 启发的声明式语言，它将大型语言模型视为生成知识库，并将其上下文窗口视为受限资源。 SPL 提供显式的WITH BUDGET/LIMIT 令牌管理、自动查询优化器、类似于SQL 的EXPLAIN ANALYZE 的EXPLAIN 透明度，以及检索增强生成(RAG) 和持久内存在单个声明性框架中的本机集成。 SPL-flow 通过对 .spl 脚本完全透明的三层提供程序回退策略（Ollama -> OpenRouter -> 自愈重试）将 SPL 扩展到弹性代理管道中。五个扩展展示了该范例的广度：（1）Text2SPL（多语言 NL->SPL 翻译）； (2) 模型混合 (MoM) 路由，在运行时将每个 PROMPT 分派给领域专家模型； (3) 逻辑分块，一种针对超过单个上下文窗口的文档的智能策略——通过 SPL 现有的 CTE 语法自然地表达，无需新的构造，将大型查询分解为 Map-Reduce 管道，将注意力成本从 O(N^2) 降低到 O(N^2/k)，并在云（并行）或本地硬件（顺序）上相同地运行； (4) SPL-flow，一个声明式代理编排层，具有弹性的三层提供程序后备； (5) 用于并行多模型比较和自动获胜者持久性的 BENCHMARK。我们提供正式的 EBNF 语法、两个可通过 pip 安装的 Python 包（spl-llm、spl-flow），以及与 Prompty、DSPy 和 LMQL 的比较。 SPL 平均将提示样板文件减少 65%，将跨模型层的 68 倍成本分散作为预执行信号，并在 OpenRouter 上以 0.002 美元运行相同的 .spl 脚本，或者在本地 Ollama 实例上以零边际成本运行相同的 .spl 脚本，无需修改。

</details>

---



</details>

# arXiv Agent Papers - 2026-02-25

**Paper Count**: 19
